**[AMENDED AND RESTATED CERTIFICATE OF INCORPORATION AND AMENDED AND RESTATED BYLAWS OF CRESTLINE SOFTWARE, INC.]{.underline}**

**[PART ONE: AMENDED AND RESTATED CERTIFICATE OF INCORPORATION OF CRESTLINE SOFTWARE, INC.]{.underline}**

**Preamble / Recitals**

Crestline Software, Inc., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware (the \"Corporation\"), was originally incorporated under the name \"Crestline Software, Inc.\" by filing its original Certificate of Incorporation with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on March 14, 2017. This Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation (the \"Restated Certificate\") has been duly adopted in accordance with Sections 242 and 245 of the General Corporation Law of the State of Delaware (the \"DGCL\") and shall become effective upon the closing of the Corporation\'s initial public offering of its Class A Common Stock (the \"IPO Closing\").

This Restated Certificate restates, integrates, and amends the Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation, as heretofore amended, in its entirety. From and after the IPO Closing, the provisions set forth in this Restated Certificate shall become the Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation.

The text of the Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation is hereby amended and restated to read in its entirety as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- NAME]{.underline}**

The name of the corporation is Crestline Software, Inc. (the \"Corporation\").

**[ARTICLE II --- REGISTERED OFFICE AND AGENT]{.underline}**

The address of the registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware is Corporation Trust Center, 1209 Orange Street, Wilmington, New Castle County, Delaware 19801. The name of the registered agent of the Corporation at such address is Capitol Corporate Services, Inc.

**[ARTICLE III --- PURPOSE]{.underline}**

The purpose of the Corporation is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the DGCL.

**[ARTICLE IV --- AUTHORIZED CAPITAL STOCK]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Authorized Shares**

The total number of shares of all classes of stock that the Corporation shall have authority to issue is 1,500,000,000 shares, consisting of: (a) 1,200,000,000 shares of Class A Common Stock, par value \$0.0001 per share (\"Class A Common Stock\"); (b) 200,000,000 shares of Class B Common Stock, par value \$0.0001 per share (\"Class B Common Stock\"); and (c) 100,000,000 shares of Preferred Stock, par value \$0.0001 per share (\"Preferred Stock\"). The Class A Common Stock and the Class B Common Stock are referred to collectively herein as the \"Common Stock.\"

**Section 4.2 --- Class A Common Stock**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, the holders of Class A Common Stock shall have the following rights, powers, preferences, and privileges.

Each holder of shares of Class A Common Stock shall be entitled to one (1) vote for each such share held of record on all matters submitted to a vote of the stockholders of the Corporation, whether voting separately as a class or together with one or more other classes, except as otherwise required by law or this Restated Certificate.

Subject to applicable law and to the rights, if any, of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding having dividend rights, the holders of Class A Common Stock shall be entitled to receive, when, as, and if declared by the Board of Directors out of funds legally available therefor, dividends and other distributions in cash, stock, or property of the Corporation. Any such dividends or distributions shall be payable to the holders of Class A Common Stock and Class B Common Stock on an equal per share basis; provided, however, that in the event a dividend or distribution payable in shares of Class A Common Stock is made to holders of Class A Common Stock, the corresponding dividend or distribution payable to holders of Class B Common Stock may be made in shares of Class B Common Stock, and in the event a dividend or distribution payable in shares of Class B Common Stock is made to holders of Class B Common Stock, the corresponding dividend or distribution payable to holders of Class A Common Stock may be made in shares of Class A Common Stock, in each case in such manner as preserves the relative rights of the classes of Common Stock.

In the event of any voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution, distribution of assets, or winding up of the Corporation, after payment in full of the debts and other liabilities of the Corporation and the payment to or setting apart for the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding of any amounts to which they may be entitled, the remaining assets of the Corporation available for distribution to stockholders shall be distributed ratably among the holders of Class A Common Stock and the holders of Class B Common Stock on an equal per share basis.

The holders of Class A Common Stock shall not have any conversion, exchange, or redemption rights, except as otherwise expressly provided by this Restated Certificate or as required by law. Shares of Class A Common Stock shall be fully paid and nonassessable.

Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Restated Certificate, the DGCL, or by the terms of any series of Preferred Stock, the holders of Class A Common Stock shall possess all rights incident to the ownership of common stock in a Delaware corporation.

**Section 4.3 --- Class B Common Stock**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, the holders of Class B Common Stock shall have the following rights, powers, preferences, and privileges.

Each holder of shares of Class B Common Stock shall be entitled to ten (10) votes for each such share held of record on all matters submitted to a vote of the stockholders of the Corporation, whether voting separately as a class or together with one or more other classes, except as otherwise required by law or this Restated Certificate.

Subject to applicable law and to the rights, if any, of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding having dividend rights, the holders of Class B Common Stock shall be entitled to receive, when, as, and if declared by the Board of Directors out of funds legally available therefor, dividends and other distributions in cash, stock, or property of the Corporation on an equal per share basis with the holders of Class A Common Stock; provided, however, that in the event a dividend or distribution payable in shares of Class A Common Stock is made to holders of Class A Common Stock, the corresponding dividend or distribution payable to holders of Class B Common Stock may be made in shares of Class B Common Stock, and in the event a dividend or distribution payable in shares of Class B Common Stock is made to holders of Class B Common Stock, the corresponding dividend or distribution payable to holders of Class A Common Stock may be made in shares of Class A Common Stock, in each case in such manner as preserves the relative rights of the classes of Common Stock.

In the event of any voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution, distribution of assets, or winding up of the Corporation, after payment in full of the debts and other liabilities of the Corporation and the payment to or setting apart for the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding of any amounts to which they may be entitled, the remaining assets of the Corporation available for distribution to stockholders shall be distributed ratably among the holders of Class A Common Stock and the holders of Class B Common Stock on an equal per share basis.

Each share of Class B Common Stock shall be convertible, at any time and from time to time, at the option of the holder thereof, into one (1) fully paid and nonassessable share of Class A Common Stock. Such right of conversion shall be exercised by the surrender of the certificate or certificates representing, or if uncertificated, by book-entry transfer of, the shares of Class B Common Stock to be converted in the manner provided by the Corporation, accompanied by written notice to the Corporation that the holder elects to convert such shares. Conversion shall be deemed effective as of the close of business on the date such surrender and notice are received, and such conversion shall be irrevocable.

Each outstanding share of Class B Common Stock shall automatically, without any further action by the Corporation or the holder thereof, convert into one (1) share of Class A Common Stock upon the occurrence of any of the following events:

> \(a\) the Transfer of such share to any person or entity other than a Permitted Transferee; or
>
> \(b\) the death or Incapacity of the holder thereof, if such holder is a natural person; provided that any shares of Class B Common Stock then held by any Permitted Transferee of such holder shall also automatically convert into shares of Class A Common Stock upon such death or Incapacity.

For purposes of this Section 4.3, \"Transfer\" means, with respect to a share of Class B Common Stock, any direct or indirect sale, assignment, transfer, conveyance, hypothecation, pledge, encumbrance, or other disposition, whether voluntary or involuntary, by operation of law or otherwise, of such share or any legal or beneficial interest therein; provided, however, that the following shall not be considered a Transfer: (i) the granting of a revocable proxy to officers or directors of the Corporation at the request of the Board of Directors in connection with actions to be taken at an annual or special meeting of stockholders; (ii) the pledge of shares of Class B Common Stock by a stockholder that creates a mere security interest in such shares pursuant to a bona fide loan or indebtedness transaction, so long as such stockholder continues to exercise voting control over such pledged shares unless and until the lender or pledgee forecloses upon such shares; and (iii) the entry into a voting trust, agreement, or arrangement pursuant to which the voting rights with respect to such shares are retained by the holder.

For purposes of this Section 4.3, \"Permitted Transferee\" means, with respect to any holder of Class B Common Stock:

> \(a\) such holder\'s spouse, domestic partner, parents, children, grandchildren, and trusts established for the primary benefit of any of the foregoing persons;
>
> \(b\) any corporation, partnership, limited liability company, trust, foundation, or other entity that is wholly owned, directly or indirectly, by such holder or by one or more Permitted Transferees of such holder; and
>
> \(c\) transfers by Arun Ramaswamy to Katerina Litvak, and transfers by Katerina Litvak to Arun Ramaswamy.

For purposes of this Section 4.3, \"Incapacity\" means that such holder has been determined by a court of competent jurisdiction to be legally incompetent or incapable of managing such holder\'s affairs.

Upon any conversion of shares of Class B Common Stock into shares of Class A Common Stock, the Corporation shall at all times reserve and keep available out of its authorized but unissued shares of Class A Common Stock such number of shares of Class A Common Stock as shall from time to time be sufficient to effect the conversion of all outstanding shares of Class B Common Stock.

The Corporation may, from time to time, establish such policies and procedures, not inconsistent with the provisions of this Restated Certificate, relating to the conversion of Class B Common Stock into Class A Common Stock, including with respect to uncertificated shares and notices of conversion, as the Board of Directors may determine to be necessary or advisable to facilitate the administration of this Section 4.3.

Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Restated Certificate with respect to voting rights and conversion rights, the powers, preferences, and relative, participating, optional, and other special rights of the Class B Common Stock, and the qualifications, limitations, and restrictions thereof, shall be identical in all respects to those of the Class A Common Stock.

**Section 4.4 --- Preferred Stock**

The Board of Directors is expressly authorized, subject to any limitations prescribed by the DGCL and this Restated Certificate, by resolution or resolutions adopted from time to time, to provide for the issuance of shares of Preferred Stock in one or more series, and, with respect to each such series, to fix the designation of such series, the number of shares constituting such series, and the powers, preferences, and rights, and the qualifications, limitations, and restrictions thereof, including, without limitation, dividend rights, dividend rates, conversion rights, voting rights, terms of redemption, redemption prices, sinking fund provisions, liquidation preferences, and such other rights and preferences as may be permitted by law.

The authority of the Board of Directors with respect to each series of Preferred Stock shall include, but not be limited to, determination of the following:

> \(a\) the designation of the series;
>
> \(b\) the number of shares constituting the series, which number the Board of Directors may thereafter increase or decrease, but not below the number of shares thereof then outstanding;
>
> \(c\) whether dividends shall be cumulative and, if so, from which date or dates, and the rate or amount of dividends;
>
> \(d\) whether the shares shall be redeemable and, if so, the terms and conditions of redemption, including the date or dates upon or after which they shall be redeemable and the amount per share payable in case of redemption;
>
> \(e\) the amounts payable upon shares of the series in the event of any voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Corporation;
>
> \(f\) whether the shares shall be convertible into or exchangeable for shares of any other class or series, or any other security, and, if so, the specification of such other class or series or such other security, the conversion or exchange price or prices or rate or rates, any adjustments thereof, and all other terms and conditions upon which such conversion or exchange may be made;
>
> \(g\) restrictions on the issuance of shares of the same series or of any other class or series; and
>
> \(h\) the voting rights, if any, of the holders of such series.

Upon the IPO Closing, all previously outstanding shares of the Corporation\'s Series A Preferred Stock, Series B Preferred Stock, Series C Preferred Stock, and Series D Preferred Stock shall have been converted into shares of Class A Common Stock on a one-for-one basis in accordance with their respective terms. Specifically, 8,000,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock, 9,500,000 shares of Series B Preferred Stock, 7,916,667 shares of Series C Preferred Stock, and 10,000,000 shares of Series D Preferred Stock, for an aggregate of 35,416,667 shares of Preferred Stock, shall have converted into an aggregate of 35,416,667 shares of Class A Common Stock at the IPO Closing.

From and after the effectiveness of this Restated Certificate, the designations, powers, preferences, and rights of the Series A Preferred Stock, Series B Preferred Stock, Series C Preferred Stock, and Series D Preferred Stock, and the qualifications, limitations, and restrictions thereof, shall be eliminated in all respects, and no shares of such series shall remain outstanding. No shares of Preferred Stock are outstanding as of the effectiveness of this Restated Certificate.

**Section 4.5 --- Rights of Holders of Common Stock Generally**

Except as otherwise required by law or this Restated Certificate, the holders of Class A Common Stock and the holders of Class B Common Stock shall vote together as a single class on all matters submitted to a vote of the stockholders of the Corporation, with each share of Class A Common Stock entitled to one (1) vote and each share of Class B Common Stock entitled to ten (10) votes.

In addition to any other vote required by law or this Restated Certificate, the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of the outstanding shares of Class A Common Stock or Class B Common Stock, as applicable, voting separately as a class, shall be required to amend, alter, or repeal, whether by merger, consolidation, or otherwise, any provision of this Restated Certificate if such amendment, alteration, or repeal would:

> \(a\) alter or change the powers, preferences, or special rights of the shares of such class so as to affect them adversely;
>
> \(b\) increase or decrease the authorized number of shares of such class, other than an increase or decrease that correspondingly increases or decreases the authorized number of shares of the other class of Common Stock in a manner that preserves the relative rights of the classes; or
>
> \(c\) alter, amend, or repeal any provision of this Restated Certificate relating to the voting, conversion, or other provisions specifically applicable to such class.

**[ARTICLE V --- BOARD OF DIRECTORS]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Powers**

The business and affairs of the Corporation shall be managed by or under the direction of the Board of Directors, except as may be otherwise provided by law or by this Restated Certificate.

**Section 5.2 --- Number of Directors**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding to elect additional directors under specified circumstances, the total number of directors constituting the entire Board of Directors shall be fixed exclusively by resolution adopted by a majority of the entire Board of Directors. The initial number of directors constituting the entire Board of Directors shall be seven (7).

**Section 5.3 --- Classified Board**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, the directors shall be divided into three classes, designated Class I, Class II, and Class III, as nearly equal in number as possible. Each director shall serve for a term ending on the date of the third annual meeting of stockholders following the annual meeting at which such director was elected, except as otherwise provided herein for the initial classification of the Board, and until such director\'s successor is duly elected and qualified or until such director\'s earlier death, resignation, or removal.

The initial Class I directors shall be Priya Venkatesh and Thomas J. Moreno, and their terms shall expire at the 2026 annual meeting of stockholders. The initial Class II directors shall be Dr. Helen Park and James Kwon, and their terms shall expire at the 2027 annual meeting of stockholders. The initial Class III directors shall be Arun Ramaswamy, Katerina Litvak, and Adaora Obi, and their terms shall expire at the 2028 annual meeting of stockholders.

At each annual meeting of stockholders following the effectiveness of this Restated Certificate, the successors to the class of directors whose term expires at that annual meeting shall be elected for a three-year term expiring at the third succeeding annual meeting of stockholders and until their respective successors shall have been duly elected and qualified.

**Section 5.4 --- Removal of Directors**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, any director may be removed from office only for cause and only by the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, voting together as a single class.

**Section 5.5 --- Vacancies**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, any vacancy occurring on the Board of Directors, including any vacancy resulting from death, resignation, removal, disqualification, an increase in the authorized number of directors, or any other cause, shall be filled solely by the affirmative vote of a majority of the remaining directors then in office, although less than a quorum, or by a sole remaining director. No stockholder shall have any right to fill any vacancy on the Board of Directors.

**[ARTICLE VI --- STOCKHOLDER ACTION]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- No Written Consent of Class A Holders**

Any action required or permitted to be taken by the stockholders of the Corporation must be effected at a duly called annual or special meeting of stockholders and may not be effected by written consent in lieu of a meeting; provided, however, that any action required or permitted to be taken by the holders of Class B Common Stock, voting separately as a class, may be taken without a meeting, without prior notice, and without a vote, if a consent or consents in writing, setting forth the action so taken, shall be signed by the holders of outstanding shares of Class B Common Stock having not less than the minimum number of votes that would be necessary to authorize or take such action at a meeting at which all shares entitled to vote thereon were present and voted.

**Section 6.2 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of stockholders of the Corporation may be called only by (a) the Chairman of the Board of Directors, (b) the Chief Executive Officer, or (c) the Board of Directors pursuant to a resolution adopted by a majority of the entire Board of Directors. Stockholders shall not have the power to call a special meeting of stockholders.

**[ARTICLE VII --- LIMITATION OF DIRECTOR LIABILITY]{.underline}**

To the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, as the same exists or may hereafter be amended, no director of the Corporation shall be personally liable to the Corporation or any of its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as a director. To the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, as the same exists or may hereafter be amended, no officer of the Corporation shall be personally liable to the Corporation or any of its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as an officer, including as contemplated by Section 102(b)(7) of the DGCL, as amended, except to the extent such exculpation is not permitted under the DGCL, including with respect to breaches of the duty of loyalty, acts or omissions not in good faith or involving intentional misconduct or a knowing violation of law, any transaction from which the person derived an improper personal benefit, or any action by or in the right of the Corporation in the case of officers.

If the DGCL is amended after the effective date of this Restated Certificate to authorize corporate action further eliminating or limiting the personal liability of directors or officers, then the liability of a director or officer of the Corporation shall be eliminated or limited to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, as so amended. Any repeal or modification of this Article VII by the stockholders of the Corporation shall not adversely affect any right or protection of a director or officer of the Corporation existing at the time of such repeal or modification.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- INDEMNIFICATION]{.underline}**

The Corporation shall, to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, as the same exists or may hereafter be amended, indemnify and hold harmless any person who is or was made or is threatened to be made a party to or is otherwise involved in any threatened, pending, or completed action, suit, or proceeding, whether civil, criminal, administrative, or investigative, by reason of the fact that such person, or a person for whom such person is the legal representative, is or was a director or officer of the Corporation or, while a director or officer of the Corporation, is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer, employee, or agent of another corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, nonprofit entity, employee benefit plan, or other enterprise, against all expense, liability, and loss suffered or incurred by such person in connection therewith, including attorneys\' fees, judgments, fines, excise taxes, and amounts paid in settlement.

The Corporation shall pay the expenses, including attorneys\' fees, incurred by a director or officer of the Corporation in defending any such proceeding in advance of its final disposition; provided, however, that such payment of expenses in advance of the final disposition of a proceeding shall be made only upon receipt of an undertaking by or on behalf of such person to repay all amounts so advanced if it shall ultimately be determined that such person is not entitled to be indemnified under this Article VIII or otherwise.

The rights to indemnification and advancement of expenses conferred by this Article VIII shall not be exclusive of any other rights that any person may have or hereafter acquire under this Restated Certificate, the Bylaws, any agreement, vote of stockholders or disinterested directors, or otherwise. The Corporation may, to the extent authorized from time to time by the Board of Directors, grant rights to indemnification and advancement of expenses to employees and agents of the Corporation or other persons serving the Corporation.

The Corporation shall have power to purchase and maintain insurance on behalf of any person who is or was a director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation, or is or was serving at the request of the Corporation in any such capacity for another enterprise, against any liability asserted against such person and incurred by such person in any such capacity, whether or not the Corporation would have the power to indemnify such person against such liability under the DGCL.

Any repeal or modification of this Article VIII shall not adversely affect any rights to indemnification or advancement of expenses of any person existing at the time of such repeal or modification with respect to any acts or omissions occurring prior to such repeal or modification.

**[ARTICLE IX --- AMENDMENT OF CERTIFICATE OF INCORPORATION]{.underline}**

The Corporation reserves the right to amend, alter, change, or repeal any provision contained in this Restated Certificate, in the manner now or hereafter prescribed by the DGCL, and all rights conferred upon stockholders herein are granted subject to this reservation.

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Restated Certificate or the Bylaws of the Corporation, and in addition to any other vote required by law, the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote thereon, voting together as a single class, shall be required to amend, alter, repeal, or adopt any provision inconsistent with Article V, Article VI, this Article IX, Article X, or the provisions of Article IV relating to the Class B Common Stock.

**[ARTICLE X --- EXCLUSIVE FORUM PROVISION]{.underline}**

**Section 10.1 --- Delaware Forum for State-Law Claims**

Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware shall, to the fullest extent permitted by law, be the sole and exclusive forum for:

> \(a\) any derivative action or proceeding brought on behalf of the Corporation;
>
> \(b\) any action asserting a claim of breach of a fiduciary duty owed by any director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation to the Corporation or the Corporation\'s stockholders;
>
> \(c\) any action asserting a claim arising pursuant to any provision of the DGCL, this Restated Certificate, or the Bylaws of the Corporation; or
>
> \(d\) any action asserting a claim governed by the internal affairs doctrine.

If the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware does not have jurisdiction over any such action or proceeding, then the sole and exclusive forum for such action or proceeding shall be the federal district court for the District of Delaware.

**Section 10.2 --- Federal Forum for Securities Act Claims**

Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the federal district courts of the United States of America shall, to the fullest extent permitted by law, be the sole and exclusive forum for the resolution of any complaint asserting a cause of action arising under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"Securities Act\"). This Section 10.2 shall not apply to claims brought to enforce any duty or liability created by the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, or any other claim for which the federal courts of the United States have exclusive jurisdiction.

**Section 10.3 --- Consent to Jurisdiction**

Any person or entity purchasing or otherwise acquiring or holding any interest in shares of capital stock of the Corporation shall be deemed to have notice of and consented to the provisions of this Article X.

**Section 10.4 --- Severability**

If any provision or provisions of this Article X shall be held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable as applied to any person, entity, action, suit, or proceeding, then, to the fullest extent permitted by law, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Article X, and the application of such provision or provisions to any other person, entity, action, suit, or proceeding, shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby.

**[ARTICLE XI --- CORPORATE OPPORTUNITIES]{.underline}**

To the fullest extent permitted by Section 122(17) of the DGCL, the Corporation renounces any interest or expectancy in, or in being offered an opportunity to participate in, any business opportunity that may be from time to time presented to any director of the Corporation who is also a partner, member, director, officer, employee, or agent of any investment fund, venture capital fund, private equity fund, or similar entity, including Graymont Ventures and Ridgeline Growth Partners and their respective affiliates, or any of their respective portfolio companies, investment vehicles, or affiliated funds.

No such director shall be liable to the Corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages or otherwise by reason of the fact that such director pursues or acquires such business opportunity for himself, herself, or itself, directs such business opportunity to another person or entity, or does not present such business opportunity to the Corporation, unless such business opportunity was expressly offered to such person solely in his or her capacity as a director of the Corporation.

Neither the amendment nor repeal of this Article XI, nor the adoption of any provision of this Restated Certificate inconsistent with this Article XI, shall eliminate or reduce the effect of this Article XI in respect of any business opportunity first identified or any other matter occurring prior to such amendment, repeal, or adoption.

**[ARTICLE XII --- SEVERABILITY]{.underline}**

If any provision of this Restated Certificate shall be held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, then the remaining provisions of this Restated Certificate shall remain in full force and effect to the fullest extent permitted by law.

**Certificate Execution Block**

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned has executed this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation as of \[●\], 2025.

CRESTLINE SOFTWARE, INC.

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Sandra Y. Chen

Title: Corporate Secretary

**[PART TWO: AMENDED AND RESTATED BYLAWS OF CRESTLINE SOFTWARE, INC.]{.underline}**

**Preamble.**These Amended and Restated Bylaws (the \"Bylaws\") of Crestline Software, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Corporation\"), are adopted effective as of the IPO Closing.

**[ARTICLE I --- OFFICES]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Registered Office**

The registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware shall be as set forth in the Restated Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation, as the same may be amended from time to time, or as otherwise designated by the Board of Directors.

**Section 1.2 --- Other Offices**

The Corporation may also maintain offices at such other places, both within and outside the State of Delaware, as the Board of Directors may from time to time designate or as the business of the Corporation may require. The principal executive offices of the Corporation are located at 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759.

**[ARTICLE II --- MEETINGS OF STOCKHOLDERS]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Annual Meetings**

An annual meeting of the stockholders shall be held each year on such date and at such time as may be designated by the Board of Directors and stated in the notice of the meeting. Annual meetings may be held at such place, if any, within or outside the State of Delaware, or solely by means of remote communication, as determined by the Board of Directors in accordance with Section 211(a)(2) of the DGCL.

**Section 2.2 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of the stockholders may be called only in the manner set forth in Article VI, Section 6.2 of the Restated Certificate of Incorporation. Accordingly, special meetings may be called only by the Chairman of the Board, the Chief Executive Officer, or the Board of Directors pursuant to a resolution adopted by a majority of the entire Board of Directors. Stockholders shall not have the power to call special meetings.

**Section 2.3 --- Notice of Meetings**

Whenever stockholders are required or permitted to take any action at a meeting, written notice of the meeting shall be given in accordance with Section 222 of the DGCL to each stockholder entitled to vote at such meeting. Such notice shall state the place, if any, date, and hour of the meeting, the means of remote communication, if any, by which stockholders and proxy holders may be deemed to be present and vote at such meeting, and, in the case of a special meeting, the purpose or purposes for which the meeting is called. Notice shall be given not fewer than ten (10) nor more than sixty (60) days before the date of the meeting.

**Section 2.4 --- Quorum**

Except as otherwise required by law, the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, the holders of a majority of the voting power of all outstanding shares of stock of the Corporation entitled to vote at the meeting, present in person or represented by proxy, shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business. If a quorum is not present, the chairman of the meeting may adjourn the meeting from time to time until a quorum shall be present or represented.

**Section 2.5 --- Voting**

Except as otherwise provided by law, the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, each holder of Class A Common Stock shall be entitled to one (1) vote for each share of Class A Common Stock held of record, and each holder of Class B Common Stock shall be entitled to ten (10) votes for each share of Class B Common Stock held of record, on each matter submitted to a vote of the stockholders. Except as otherwise provided by law, the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, all matters other than the election of directors shall be decided by the affirmative vote of a majority of the voting power of shares present in person or represented by proxy at the meeting and entitled to vote thereon. Directors shall be elected by a plurality of the votes cast.

**Section 2.6 --- Proxies**

Each stockholder entitled to vote at a meeting of stockholders may authorize another person or persons to act for such stockholder by proxy in any manner permitted by law. No proxy shall be voted or acted upon after three (3) years from its date, unless the proxy provides for a longer or shorter period. Every proxy shall be filed with or transmitted to the Secretary of the Corporation before or at the time of the meeting.

**Section 2.7 --- Action Without a Meeting**

Any action required or permitted to be taken at any annual or special meeting of stockholders may not be taken by written consent in lieu of a meeting, except as expressly provided in Article VI of the Restated Certificate of Incorporation with respect to action by holders of Class B Common Stock voting separately as a class.

**Section 2.8 --- Advance Notice of Stockholder Business and Nominations**

**(a) Annual Meeting Business.**At an annual meeting of the stockholders, only such business shall be conducted as shall have been properly brought before the meeting. To be properly brought before an annual meeting, business must be: (i) specified in the notice of meeting given by or at the direction of the Board of Directors; (ii) otherwise properly brought before the meeting by or at the direction of the Board of Directors; or (iii) otherwise properly brought before the meeting by a stockholder of the Corporation who (A) was a stockholder of record at the time of giving notice provided for in this Section 2.8 and at the time of the meeting, (B) is entitled to vote at the meeting, and (C) has complied with this Section 2.8 in all applicable respects. For business to be properly brought before an annual meeting by a stockholder, the stockholder must deliver timely written notice to the Secretary of the Corporation.

To be timely, a stockholder\'s notice with respect to an annual meeting must be received by the Secretary at the principal executive offices of the Corporation not earlier than the close of business on the one hundred twentieth (120th) day and not later than the close of business on the ninetieth (90th) day before the first anniversary of the preceding year\'s annual meeting; provided, however, that if the date of the annual meeting is more than thirty (30) days before or more than sixty (60) days after such anniversary date, notice by the stockholder must be so received not earlier than the close of business on the one hundred twentieth (120th) day prior to such annual meeting and not later than the close of business on the later of (i) the ninetieth (90th) day prior to such annual meeting or (ii) the tenth (10th) day following the day on which public announcement of the date of such annual meeting is first made.

**(b) Stockholder Nominations.**Only persons who are nominated in accordance with the procedures set forth in this Section 2.8 shall be eligible for election as directors, except as may otherwise be provided in the Restated Certificate of Incorporation with respect to the rights of holders of Preferred Stock. Nominations of persons for election to the Board of Directors may be made at an annual meeting of stockholders only: (i) by or at the direction of the Board of Directors; or (ii) by a stockholder who (A) was a stockholder of record at the time of giving notice provided for in this Section 2.8 and at the time of the meeting, (B) is entitled to vote for the election of directors at the meeting, and (C) has complied with the notice procedures set forth in this Section 2.8. Such stockholder\'s notice shall be timely only if delivered within the time periods specified in subsection (a) above.

**(c) Required Information in Stockholder\'s Notice --- Business Proposals.**A stockholder\'s notice of business proposals shall set forth, as to each matter the stockholder proposes to bring before the annual meeting:

> \(i\) a brief description of the business desired to be brought before the annual meeting;
>
> \(ii\) the text of the proposal or business, including the text of any resolutions proposed for consideration and, in the event that such business includes a proposal to amend these Bylaws, the language of the proposed amendment;
>
> \(iii\) the reasons for conducting such business at the annual meeting;
>
> \(iv\) any material interest of the stockholder giving the notice and any beneficial owner on whose behalf the proposal is made in such business;
>
> \(v\) the name and address, as they appear on the Corporation\'s books, of the stockholder proposing such business and of any beneficial owner on whose behalf the proposal is made;
>
> \(vi\) the class, series, and number of shares of capital stock of the Corporation that are owned beneficially and of record by such stockholder and any such beneficial owner;
>
> \(vii\) any derivative positions, hedging transactions, short interests, profit interests, options, warrants, convertible securities, stock appreciation rights, borrowed or loaned shares, or similar arrangements or understandings, whether or not entered into directly or indirectly, that have been entered into by, or on behalf of, such stockholder or beneficial owner, the effect or intent of which is to mitigate loss, manage risk, or benefit from changes in the price of the Corporation\'s securities;
>
> \(viii\) any proxy, contract, arrangement, understanding, or relationship pursuant to which such stockholder or beneficial owner has a right to vote any shares of any security of the Corporation;
>
> \(ix\) any rights to dividends on the shares of the Corporation that are separated or separable from the underlying shares of the Corporation;
>
> \(x\) any performance-related fees, other than an asset-based fee, to which such stockholder or beneficial owner may be entitled as a result of any increase or decrease in the value of shares of the Corporation or any derivative instruments based on shares of the Corporation; and
>
> \(xi\) a representation that the stockholder intends to appear in person or by proxy at the annual meeting to bring such business before the meeting.

**(d) Required Information in Stockholder\'s Notice --- Nominations.**A stockholder\'s notice of nomination of persons for election to the Board of Directors shall set forth:

> \(i\) as to each person whom the stockholder proposes to nominate for election or reelection as a director, all information relating to such person that is required to be disclosed in solicitations of proxies for election of directors under Regulation 14A under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and such person\'s written consent to being named in the proxy statement as a nominee and to serving as a director if elected;
>
> \(ii\) a completed written questionnaire, in the form required by the Corporation, with respect to the background, qualifications, stock ownership, and independence of such proposed nominee;
>
> \(iii\) a representation and description regarding the nominee\'s background, qualifications, experience, and independence, including any information necessary for the Corporation to determine the eligibility of such nominee to serve as an independent director or on any committee of the Board of Directors; and
>
> \(iv\) as to the stockholder giving the notice and any beneficial owner on whose behalf the nomination is made, all of the information required to be set forth in subsection (c) above.

**(e) Updates and Supplements.**A stockholder providing notice of business proposed to be brought before an annual meeting or notice of any nomination for election to the Board of Directors shall further update and supplement such notice, if necessary, so that the information provided or required to be provided in such notice shall be true and correct as of the record date for determining the stockholders entitled to notice of the meeting and as of the date that is ten (10) business days prior to the meeting or any adjournment or postponement thereof. Such update and supplement shall be delivered to, or mailed and received by, the Secretary at the principal executive offices of the Corporation not later than five (5) business days after the record date for the meeting and not later than eight (8) business days prior to the date for the meeting or any adjournment or postponement thereof, respectively.

**(f) Determination of Compliance.**The chairman of the meeting shall have the power and duty to determine whether any proposed business to be brought before the annual meeting or any nomination proposed to be made at the annual meeting was made or proposed, as the case may be, in accordance with the procedures set forth in these Bylaws, and if any such proposed business or nomination is not in compliance with these Bylaws, the chairman of the meeting shall declare that such defective proposal or nomination shall be disregarded.

**(g) Rule 14a-8 Savings Clause.**Nothing in these Bylaws shall be deemed to affect any rights of stockholders to request inclusion of proposals in the Corporation\'s proxy statement pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, or any successor rule.

**Section 2.9 --- Inspectors of Election**

Before any meeting of stockholders, the Board of Directors may appoint one or more inspectors of election to act at the meeting or any adjournment thereof. If no inspectors are so appointed, the chairman of the meeting may, and upon the request of any stockholder entitled to vote shall, appoint one or more inspectors. The inspectors shall perform such duties as are prescribed by the DGCL, including determining the number of shares outstanding and the voting power of each, the shares represented at the meeting, the existence of a quorum, the validity of proxies and ballots, and the results of voting.

**Section 2.10 --- List of Stockholders**

At least ten (10) days before every meeting of stockholders, the Corporation shall prepare a complete list of the stockholders entitled to vote at the meeting, arranged in alphabetical order and showing the address of, and the number of shares registered in the name of, each stockholder. Such list shall be open to the examination of any stockholder for any purpose germane to the meeting, during ordinary business hours, at the Corporation\'s principal place of business or on a reasonably accessible electronic network, as permitted by the DGCL.

**[ARTICLE III --- BOARD OF DIRECTORS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- General Powers**

The business and affairs of the Corporation shall be managed by or under the direction of the Board of Directors, subject to the provisions of the Restated Certificate of Incorporation and the DGCL.

**Section 3.2 --- Number, Qualification, Election, and Term**

The number, qualification, election, and term of directors shall be as set forth in Article V of the Restated Certificate of Incorporation. The initial number of directors shall be seven (7), and the directors shall be divided into three classes with staggered three-year terms. Directors need not be stockholders of the Corporation or residents of the State of Delaware.

**Section 3.3 --- Regular Meetings**

Regular meetings of the Board of Directors may be held without notice at such times and at such places, within or outside the State of Delaware, as shall from time to time be determined by the Board of Directors. A regular meeting of the Board of Directors may be held immediately following the annual meeting of stockholders at the same place or by the same means of communication, and no notice of such meeting shall be necessary.

**Section 3.4 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of the Board of Directors may be called by the Chairman of the Board, the Chief Executive Officer, or a majority of the total number of directors then in office. Notice of any special meeting shall be given to each director at least twenty-four (24) hours prior thereto if given by telephone, electronic mail, or personal delivery, or at least forty-eight (48) hours prior thereto if given by mail. Any such notice need not specify the purpose of the meeting.

**Section 3.5 --- Quorum and Voting**

A majority of the total number of directors then in office shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business at any meeting of the Board of Directors. Except as otherwise required by law, the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, the vote of a majority of the directors present at a meeting at which a quorum is present shall be the act of the Board of Directors.

**Section 3.6 --- Action by Written Consent**

Any action required or permitted to be taken at any meeting of the Board of Directors or of any committee thereof may be taken without a meeting if all members of the Board of Directors or such committee, as the case may be, consent thereto in writing or by electronic transmission, and such writing or writings or electronic transmission or transmissions are filed with the minutes of proceedings of the Board of Directors or committee.

**Section 3.7 --- Telephonic Meetings**

Members of the Board of Directors or any committee designated by the Board of Directors may participate in a meeting of the Board of Directors or such committee by means of conference telephone or other communications equipment by means of which all persons participating in the meeting can hear each other, and such participation shall constitute presence in person at the meeting.

**Section 3.8 --- Committees**

The Board of Directors may designate one or more committees, each committee to consist of one or more directors of the Corporation, by resolution adopted by a majority of the entire Board of Directors. Each such committee shall have and may exercise such authority of the Board of Directors as may be provided in such resolution or these Bylaws, subject to the limitations set forth in Section 141(c) of the DGCL and any other applicable law.

The Corporation shall maintain an Audit Committee, a Compensation Committee, and a Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee, together with such other committees or subcommittees as the Board of Directors may from time to time establish. The composition of each committee shall be as determined by the Board of Directors from time to time, subject to applicable law and the rules of the securities exchange on which the Corporation\'s securities are listed.

Each committee shall keep regular minutes of its meetings and report the same to the Board of Directors when required. Unless otherwise provided by the Board of Directors, a majority of the members of a committee shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business, and the act of a majority of the members present at a meeting at which a quorum is present shall be the act of the committee.

**Section 3.9 --- Compensation of Directors**

Directors shall receive such compensation for their services as directors and such reimbursement for their reasonable expenses incurred in attending meetings or otherwise in connection with the business of the Corporation as may be fixed from time to time by resolution of the Board of Directors.

**Section 3.10 --- Resignation and Removal**

Any director may resign at any time upon notice given in writing or by electronic transmission to the Corporation. Such resignation shall be effective upon delivery unless the resignation specifies a later effective date. Directors may be removed only in the manner set forth in the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, including the requirement that removal be only for cause and by the requisite supermajority vote.

**[ARTICLE IV --- OFFICERS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Designation of Officers**

The officers of the Corporation shall be elected by the Board of Directors and shall include a Chief Executive Officer, a President if appointed by the Board of Directors, a Chief Financial Officer, and a Secretary, together with such other officers as the Board of Directors may from time to time appoint, including a Chief Technology Officer, a General Counsel, one or more Vice Presidents, Assistant Secretaries, Treasurers, and such other officers as the business of the Corporation may require. Any number of offices may be held by the same person; provided, however, that no person shall simultaneously serve as both Chief Executive Officer and Secretary unless the Board of Directors otherwise determines.

The initial officers of the Corporation shall be as follows:

> •Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the Board: Arun Ramaswamy
>
> •Chief Technology Officer: Katerina \"Kate\" Litvak
>
> •Chief Financial Officer: Michael D. Hartwell
>
> •General Counsel and Corporate Secretary: Sandra Y. Chen

**Section 4.2 --- Election and Term of Office**

Each officer of the Corporation shall hold office at the pleasure of the Board of Directors and until such officer\'s successor shall have been duly elected and qualified or until such officer\'s earlier resignation, removal, death, or incapacity. Any officer may be removed by the Board of Directors at any time, with or without cause.

**Section 4.3 --- Chief Executive Officer**

The Chief Executive Officer shall have general supervision and control over the business and affairs of the Corporation, subject to the control of the Board of Directors. The Chief Executive Officer shall preside at all meetings of stockholders and, if also serving as Chairman of the Board, at all meetings of the Board of Directors. The Chief Executive Officer shall execute all authorized deeds, mortgages, bonds, contracts, or other instruments in the name of the Corporation, except in cases where the signing and execution thereof shall be expressly delegated by the Board of Directors or by these Bylaws to another officer or agent of the Corporation, and shall have such other powers and perform such other duties as may be assigned by the Board of Directors.

**Section 4.4 --- Chief Financial Officer**

The Chief Financial Officer shall have custody of the corporate funds and securities, shall keep full and accurate accounts of receipts and disbursements in books belonging to the Corporation, and shall deposit all moneys and other valuable effects in the name and to the credit of the Corporation in such depositories as may be designated by the Board of Directors. The Chief Financial Officer shall render to the Board of Directors, whenever requested, an account of the financial condition of the Corporation and shall have such other powers and perform such other duties as may be assigned by the Board of Directors or the Chief Executive Officer.

**Section 4.5 --- Secretary**

The Secretary shall keep the minutes of all meetings of the Board of Directors and of the stockholders in books provided for that purpose, shall give or cause to be given notice of all meetings required by these Bylaws or by law, shall be custodian of the corporate seal and the records of the Corporation, and shall authenticate records of the Corporation as required. Sandra Y. Chen shall serve as the Corporate Secretary of the Corporation.

**Section 4.6 --- Other Officers**

Other officers of the Corporation shall have such authority and shall perform such duties as may be prescribed from time to time by the Board of Directors or the Chief Executive Officer.

**[ARTICLE V --- STOCK]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Stock Certificates and Uncertificated Shares**

The shares of the Corporation may be represented by certificates, provided that the Board of Directors may provide by resolution or resolutions that some or all of any or all classes or series of stock shall be uncertificated shares. All shares of Class A Common Stock shall be issued in uncertificated, book-entry form unless the Board of Directors otherwise determines. Any certificates representing shares of stock shall be in such form as approved by the Board of Directors and shall be signed as required by law. The transfer agent and registrar for the Corporation\'s stock shall be Computershare Transfer Co., or such other transfer agent and registrar as the Board of Directors may designate from time to time.

**Section 5.2 --- Transfer of Stock**

Transfers of shares of stock of the Corporation shall be made only on the books of the Corporation by the holder of record thereof, by such holder\'s attorney duly authorized in writing, or by such holder\'s legal representative. If shares are certificated, transfer shall be made upon surrender of the certificate or certificates therefor, properly endorsed. If shares are uncertificated, transfer shall be made upon receipt of proper transfer instructions by the transfer agent. The Corporation shall be entitled to treat the registered holder of any share as the owner thereof for all purposes.

**Section 5.3 --- Record Date**

In order that the Corporation may determine the stockholders entitled to notice of or to vote at any meeting of stockholders or any adjournment thereof, or entitled to receive payment of any dividend or other distribution, or in order to make a determination of stockholders for any other proper purpose, the Board of Directors may fix, in advance, a record date, which shall not precede the date upon which the resolution fixing the record date is adopted by the Board of Directors. Such record date shall not be more than sixty (60) days nor less than ten (10) days before the date of such meeting, nor more than sixty (60) days prior to any other action.

**Section 5.4 --- Lost, Stolen, or Destroyed Certificates**

The Board of Directors may direct that a new certificate or uncertificated shares be issued in place of any certificate previously issued by the Corporation alleged to have been lost, stolen, or destroyed, upon the making of an affidavit of that fact by the person claiming the certificate to be lost, stolen, or destroyed and, if required by the Board of Directors, the posting of an indemnity bond in such form and amount as the Board of Directors may direct.

**[ARTICLE VI --- INDEMNIFICATION AND ADVANCEMENT]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Indemnification of Directors and Officers**

The Corporation shall indemnify any person who was or is a party or is threatened to be made a party to, or is otherwise involved in, any threatened, pending, or completed action, suit, or proceeding, whether civil, criminal, administrative, or investigative, by reason of the fact that such person is or was a director or officer of the Corporation, to the fullest extent authorized by the DGCL, as the same exists or may hereafter be amended.

In the case of any action, suit, or proceeding other than an action by or in the right of the Corporation, the Corporation shall indemnify such person against expenses, including attorneys\' fees, judgments, fines, and amounts paid in settlement actually and reasonably incurred by such person in connection with such action, suit, or proceeding, if such person acted in good faith and in a manner such person reasonably believed to be in or not opposed to the best interests of the Corporation and, with respect to any criminal action or proceeding, had no reasonable cause to believe such person\'s conduct was unlawful.

In the case of an action by or in the right of the Corporation, the Corporation shall indemnify such person against expenses, including attorneys\' fees, actually and reasonably incurred by such person in connection with the defense or settlement of such action, if such person acted in good faith and in a manner such person reasonably believed to be in or not opposed to the best interests of the Corporation, except that no indemnification shall be made in respect of any claim, issue, or matter as to which such person shall have been adjudged liable to the Corporation unless and only to the extent that the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware or the court in which such action or suit was brought shall determine that, despite the adjudication of liability, such person is fairly and reasonably entitled to indemnity for such expenses.

**Section 6.2 --- Advancement of Expenses**

The Corporation shall advance expenses, including attorneys\' fees, incurred by a director or officer in defending any threatened, pending, or completed action, suit, or proceeding in advance of the final disposition of such action, suit, or proceeding; provided, however, that such payment of expenses in advance of the final disposition of such action, suit, or proceeding shall be made only upon receipt of an undertaking by or on behalf of such director or officer to repay such amount if it shall ultimately be determined that such person is not entitled to be indemnified by the Corporation as authorized in this Article VI.

**Section 6.3 --- Non-Exclusivity**

The rights to indemnification and advancement of expenses conferred in this Article VI shall not be exclusive of any other rights that any person may have or hereafter acquire under any statute, provision of the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, these Bylaws, agreement, vote of stockholders or disinterested directors, or otherwise. The Corporation may provide additional indemnification and advancement rights by agreement, insurance, vote of stockholders or disinterested directors, or otherwise.

**Section 6.4 --- Insurance**

The Corporation may purchase and maintain insurance on behalf of any person who is or was a director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation against any liability asserted against such person and incurred by such person in any such capacity, whether or not the Corporation would have the power to indemnify such person against such liability under the DGCL.

**Section 6.5 --- Indemnification of Employees and Agents**

The Corporation may, to the extent authorized from time to time by the Board of Directors, indemnify employees and agents of the Corporation to the same extent as directors and officers or to such lesser extent as the Board of Directors may determine.

**[ARTICLE VII --- GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Dividends**

Dividends upon the capital stock of the Corporation, subject to the provisions of the Restated Certificate of Incorporation and applicable law, may be declared by the Board of Directors at any regular or special meeting out of funds legally available therefor. Any such dividends shall be subject to any preferential dividend rights of any outstanding Preferred Stock and to the equal per-share treatment of Class A Common Stock and Class B Common Stock.

**Section 7.2 --- Fiscal Year**

The fiscal year of the Corporation shall end on December 31 of each year unless otherwise determined by resolution of the Board of Directors.

**Section 7.3 --- Seal**

The corporate seal, if any, shall be in such form as approved by the Board of Directors.

**Section 7.4 --- Electronic Transmission**

Any notice, consent, waiver, proxy, appointment, or other communication permitted or required under the DGCL or these Bylaws may be given by electronic transmission to the fullest extent permitted by Section 232 of the DGCL.

**Section 7.5 --- Construction**

Whenever the context so requires, the singular shall include the plural and the plural shall include the singular. Headings used in these Bylaws are for convenience only and shall not affect the interpretation of these Bylaws.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS]{.underline}**

These Bylaws may be amended, altered, or repealed, and new bylaws may be adopted, by the Board of Directors by the affirmative vote of a majority of the entire Board of Directors then in office.

These Bylaws may also be amended, altered, or repealed by the stockholders of the Corporation; provided, however, that, notwithstanding any other provision of these Bylaws or the Restated Certificate of Incorporation, the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote thereon, voting together as a single class, shall be required for any amendment, alteration, repeal, or adoption of any provision inconsistent with Article II, Section 2.8, Article III, Section 3.8, or this Article VIII.

The Board of Directors may adopt bylaws not inconsistent with the Restated Certificate of Incorporation or the DGCL.

**Bylaws Execution / Adoption Block**

These Amended and Restated Bylaws were adopted by the Board of Directors of Crestline Software, Inc. on \[●\], 2025, effective as of the IPO Closing.

CERTIFIED BY: Sandra Y. Chen, Corporate Secretary

**[SUMMARY OF PLACEMENT]{.underline}**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Issue**                                                                             **Where in Document**                                                                                                                                                                                           **How Manifested**
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  *\**\* --- Dual-Class Sunset Provision Absence                                        Article IV, Section 4.3 (Class B Common Stock) of the Restated Certificate                                                                                                                                      The automatic conversion triggers for Class B shares include ONLY (i) transfer to non-Permitted Transferees and (ii) death/incapacity. There is NO time-based sunset (e.g., 7, 10, or 12 years from IPO) and NO ownership-percentage-based sunset. The absence is conspicuous when compared to market norms.

  *\**\* --- Controlled Company Exemption Disclosure                                    Article IV Section 4.3 (10:1 voting), Article V Section 5.3 (classified board), Article VI Section 6.1 (no written consent for Class A), Article III Section 3.8 of Bylaws (permissive committee composition)   The charter establishes the structural prerequisites for controlled company status: 10:1 voting ratio giving founders 71.8% voting power, combined with prohibition on stockholder written consent, classified board, and permissive (non-mandatory-independence) committee composition in bylaws. The documents do not explicitly reference the Nasdaq controlled company exemption --- that analysis belongs in the S-1 and issues memo --- but the structural provisions enabling it are clearly present.

  *\**\* --- Forum Selection Clause Federal Court Carve-Out for Securities Act Claims   Article X, Section 10.2 of the Restated Certificate                                                                                                                                                             The provision designates \"the federal district courts of the United States of America\" as the exclusive forum for Securities Act claims. This is permissible post-*Salzberg v. Sciabacucchi* (Del. 2020) but carries implications for Securities Act Section 11 claims that would otherwise be concurrently available in state courts under *Cyan Inc. v. Beaver County Employees Retirement Fund* (2018). The charter text itself is the provision; analysis of enforceability and disclosure implications belongs in the S-1 and issues memo.
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**CONFIDENTIAL**

**Director and Officer Questionnaire Responses** **Compiled for Form S-1 Registration Statement**

**CRESTLINE SOFTWARE, INC.**

Prepared by: **Thorncroft & Wexler LLP** 610 Lexington Avenue, 38th Floor New York, NY 10022

Lead Partner: Sarah K. Norden Associate: James T. Okafor

February 2025

CIK: 0002014387 EIN: 84-3291074

For use by outside counsel in preparing the initial draft Registration Statement on Form S-1 to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

**CONFIDENTIALITY LEGEND:** This document is protected by the attorney-client privilege and the work product doctrine. It contains confidential information compiled from Director and Officer Questionnaire responses submitted by the executive officers and directors of Crestline Software, Inc. Distribution is restricted to members of the IPO working group. All responses are subject to verification against Company records and stock transfer agent data. Do not distribute, copy, or transmit without the express written authorization of Thorncroft & Wexler LLP.

Questionnaires were distributed to all executive officers and directors of Crestline Software, Inc., a Delaware C-corporation incorporated on March 14, 2017, headquartered at 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759. Completed questionnaires were received from nine respondents (four executive officers and five non-employee directors). Responses have been compiled, organized, and annotated by counsel as set forth herein.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[Section 1 --- Instructions and General Information]{.underline}**

The Director and Officer Questionnaire was distributed to collect biographical, ownership, related-party, and governance information from each executive officer and director of Crestline Software, Inc. (the \"Company\") for use in the following sections of the prospectus to be included in the Company\'s Registration Statement on Form S-1: Management, Executive Compensation, Principal Stockholders, Certain Relationships and Related Party Transactions, Description of Capital Stock, and Risk Factors.

Each respondent was asked to provide current information as of December 31, 2024 (or a more recent practicable date) and to disclose all material facts relevant to the Company\'s disclosure obligations under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Regulation S-K, including without limitation Items 401 (Directors, Executive Officers, Promoters), 402 (Executive Compensation), 403 (Security Ownership of Certain Beneficial Owners and Management), 404 (Certain Relationships and Related Transactions), and 407 (Corporate Governance).

**Proposed Offering Terms.** The Company proposes to list its Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol \"CRST.\" The anticipated price range for the initial public offering is \$22.00 to \$26.00 per share. The Company\'s Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, to become effective immediately prior to the closing of the IPO, will authorize a dual-class common stock structure consisting of Class A common stock (one vote per share) and Class B common stock (ten votes per share). Class B common stock will be held exclusively by co-founders Arun Ramaswamy and Katerina Litvanova.

**Controlled Company Inquiry.** Each director was asked to confirm their understanding of the following: \"Please confirm whether you are aware that the Company may qualify as a \'controlled company\' under applicable Nasdaq listing rules following the IPO, given that the co-founders are expected to hold approximately 71.8% of total voting power through Class B common stock. If so, please indicate your understanding of any governance exemptions that may apply.\" This inquiry was included at the direction of lead counsel Sarah K. Norden to ensure that each director\'s understanding of the controlled company analysis is documented for purposes of downstream S-1 drafting and corporate governance disclosure.

**[Section 2 --- Executive Officer Questionnaire Responses]{.underline}**

**[2.1 --- Arun Ramaswamy (Co-Founder, Chief Executive Officer, and Chairman of the Board)]{.underline}**

**Full Name:** Arun Ramaswamy **Age:** 41 **Position:** Co-Founder, Chief Executive Officer, and Chairman of the Board, since the Company\'s incorporation on March 14, 2017 **Business Address:** 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759

**Background.** Prior to founding Crestline, Mr. Ramaswamy served as an Engagement Manager at McKinsey & Company, where he focused on technology and operations strategy engagements. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and a Master of Business Administration from the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

**Other Directorships (Last 5 Years).** None other than Crestline Software, Inc.

**Family Relationships.** None with any other officer or director of the Company.

**Legal Proceedings (Item 401(f)).** None to disclose.

**Beneficial Ownership (as of December 31, 2024):**

> • *Common Stock:* 12,200,000 shares of common stock held directly, to be designated as Class B common stock (ten votes per share) upon effectiveness of the Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation at IPO
>
> • *Stock Options:* Options to purchase additional shares of common stock (vested and exercisable) per Company equity plan records
>
> • *RSUs:* 180,000 restricted stock unit shares granted in fiscal year 2024, vesting over four years, subject to the vesting schedule approved by the Compensation Committee (45,000 shares subject to vesting as of December 31, 2024)
>
> • *Fully Diluted Ownership:* 11.6% (per Company records, based on 105,366,667 fully diluted shares outstanding)
>
> • *Class B Designation:* All 12,200,000 shares of common stock to be designated Class B upon IPO. Combined with Ms. Litvak\'s 8,600,000 Class B shares, total Class B shares will equal 20,800,000, representing voting power of (20,800,000 × 10) / ((81,616,667 × 1) + (20,800,000 × 10)) = 208,000,000 / 289,616,667 = approximately 71.8% of total voting power

**Controlled Company Analysis.**

Following the IPO, co-founders Ramaswamy and Litvak will hold an aggregate of 20,800,000 shares of Class B common stock with ten votes per share, representing 208,000,000 votes. Combined with 81,616,667 Class A shares (one vote each), total outstanding votes will equal 289,616,667. The founders\' combined voting power will be approximately 71.8%.

Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5615(c), a company of which more than 50% of the voting power is held by an individual, group, or another company is a \"controlled company\" and may elect to be exempt from: (a) the requirement that a majority of the board of directors be comprised of independent directors; (b) the requirement for a fully independent compensation committee; and (c) the requirement for a fully independent nominating committee.

**The questionnaire responses do not contain a definitive Board resolution regarding reliance on the controlled company exemption.** Mr. Ramaswamy\'s response is supportive but noncommittal. Ms. Obi recommends careful consideration and disclosure. Other directors defer to counsel or to the Board\'s collective determination.

*\[NTD: The Board has not yet formally determined whether Crestline will rely on the controlled company exemption. Given that only 3 of 6 directors are independent (50%, not a majority), reliance on at least the majority-independent-board exemption appears likely unless an additional independent director is appointed before the IPO. Counsel to schedule Board discussion and obtain formal resolution. For S-1 drafting, assume the Company WILL rely on the controlled company exemption and include appropriate risk factor disclosure regarding: (i) the absence of a majority-independent board, (ii) the potential absence of fully independent compensation and nominating committees, and (iii) the risk that investors will have limited ability to influence corporate governance.\]*
