**[8.2 --- Counsel Notes and Open Items Summary]{.underline}**

The following open items have been identified by Thorncroft & Wexler LLP (Sarah K. Norden / James T. Okafor) during compilation of questionnaire responses and require resolution before the Form S-1 is filed:

**\[OPEN ITEM 1 --- Controlled Company Exemption Election\]**

No Board resolution is on record regarding reliance on the controlled company exemption under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5615(c). The questionnaire responses reflect awareness of the dual-class structure and the resulting 71.8% voting power held by the co-founders, but no respondent provides a definitive statement as to whether the Company will elect to rely on the exemption. Current board composition (3 of 6 directors independent, or 50%) indicates that reliance on at least the majority-independent-board exemption is necessary unless the Board is expanded to include an additional independent director prior to IPO. Counsel to schedule Board discussion and obtain formal resolution. S-1 risk factor drafting will proceed on the assumption that the exemption is elected.

**\[OPEN ITEM 2 --- Vantara Logistics Related Party Disclosure Inconsistency\]**

Ms. Venkatesh disclosed the Vantara Logistics SaaS license agreement (\$1.2 million annual contract value, 3-year term, entered into April 2023) as a related party transaction, with full detail regarding Audit Committee approval and her recusal from the Board vote. Mr. Hartwell did not identify this transaction as a related party transaction in his questionnaire response. The management roadshow presentation and CFO IPO readiness memorandum treat Vantara Logistics as a standard customer contributing to ARR growth without related-party notation. All investor-facing materials must be updated to reflect Item 404 disclosure requirements. Counsel to confirm arm\'s-length pricing determination with the Audit Committee and obtain supporting documentation.

**\[OPEN ITEM 3 --- Aldersgate Capital Beneficial Ownership Gap\]**

Aldersgate Capital is identified as a Series B participant (April 2020) in the historical capitalization records and is referenced in Ms. Venkatesh\'s questionnaire response, but Aldersgate Capital does not appear in any current beneficial ownership disclosure. No questionnaire respondent reports Aldersgate Capital as a current stockholder. Possible explanations include: (a) Aldersgate Capital sold its shares in a secondary transaction; (b) Aldersgate Capital\'s stake has been diluted below 5% through subsequent financing rounds; or (c) the omission is an error. Counsel must request: (i) confirmation from the Company\'s stock transfer records (Oakvale Transfer Transfer Co.) of Aldersgate Capital\'s current holdings, if any; (ii) copies of any secondary transfer agreements involving Aldersgate Capital; and (iii) if Aldersgate Capital holds 5% or more of any class of securities, a completed D&O questionnaire or equivalent disclosure from Aldersgate Capital. The S-1 principal stockholders table (Item 403) cannot be finalized until this matter is resolved.

**\[OPEN ITEM 4 --- Director Ages\]**

Ages for non-employee directors Venkatesh, Moreno, Park, Kwon, and Obi were not provided in all questionnaire responses. Item 401 of Regulation S-K requires disclosure of the age of each director and executive officer. Counsel to follow up with each director to confirm.

**\[OPEN ITEM 5 --- Lock-Up Agreement Coverage for Fund Entities\]**

Mr. Kwon\'s questionnaire response indicates that Crestline shares are held at the fund level (Ridgeline Growth Partners Fund V, LP), not in his individual name. The same structure applies to Ms. Venkatesh and the Graymont Ventures fund entities. Lock-up agreements must bind the fund-level entities that hold the shares, not merely the individual directors who serve as representatives on the Board. Counsel to cross-reference with the underwriting agreement and lock-up letter forms to ensure proper coverage.

result:
**[EXECUTIVE SUMMARY --- TERM LOAN AND SECURITY AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Dated as of December 15, 2022**

**Crestline Software, Inc.,** as Borrower

and

**Kestridge West Credit Partners,** as Lender and Administrative Agent

PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL --- FOR INTERNAL USE ONLY

Prepared for internal use by Sandra Y. Chen, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, Crestline Software, Inc.

This summary is prepared for the benefit of management and outside counsel in connection with the Company\'s contemplated initial public offering and is not a substitute for the full Term Loan and Security Agreement dated as of December 15, 2022 (the \"Credit Agreement\"), by and between Crestline Software, Inc. and Kestridge West Credit Partners. All capitalized terms used but not otherwise defined herein have the meanings ascribed to them in the Credit Agreement. In the event of any conflict between this summary and the Credit Agreement, the Credit Agreement shall govern.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[Section 1: Overview of the Facility]{.underline}**

**Lender:** Kestridge West Credit Partners (\"SVCP\").

**Borrower:** Crestline Software, Inc., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with its principal executive offices located at 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759.

**Guarantors:** Crestline Software UK Ltd., a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, provides a limited guarantee of the Borrower\'s obligations under the Credit Agreement, secured by a first-priority lien on its assets, subject to a specified cap as described further in Section 4 below.

**Facility Type:** Senior secured term loan facility.

**Aggregate Commitment:** \$50,000,000 (Fifty Million Dollars).

**Amount Drawn as of December 31, 2024:** \$40,000,000 (Forty Million Dollars). The initial draw of \$40,000,000 was made on the closing date.

**Undrawn Commitment:** \$10,000,000. The availability period for additional draws under the facility expired on June 30, 2024. Accordingly, the remaining \$10.0 million of commitment is no longer available for borrowing and the Borrower\'s right to make additional draws has terminated.

**Closing Date of Credit Agreement:** December 15, 2022.

**Maturity Date:** December 15, 2027.

**Use of Proceeds:** General corporate purposes, including working capital, capital expenditures, and permitted acquisitions (as defined in the Credit Agreement).

**[Section 2: Interest Rate and Fees]{.underline}**

**Interest Rate.** The outstanding principal balance of the term loan bears interest at a rate per annum equal to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (\"SOFR\") plus 3.25%, subject to a SOFR floor of 1.00%. Interest is computed on the basis of a 360-day year for the actual number of days elapsed during each interest period.

**Interest Payment Dates.** Interest is payable quarterly in arrears on the last business day of each March, June, September, and December.

**Default Rate.** Upon the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default, interest on all outstanding obligations shall accrue at a rate equal to an additional 2.00% per annum above the otherwise applicable interest rate.

**Commitment Fee.** The Borrower was required to pay a commitment fee equal to 0.25% per annum on the daily average undrawn portion of the aggregate commitment, payable quarterly in arrears. Because the availability period for additional draws expired on June 30, 2024, the commitment fee is no longer accruing.

**Upfront Fee.** An upfront fee of 0.50% of the aggregate commitment (\$250,000) was paid by the Borrower to the Lender on the closing date.

**Exit Fee.** An exit fee equal to 1.00% of the outstanding principal balance at the time of repayment or maturity is due upon repayment in full or at maturity. Based on the current drawn amount of \$40,000,000, the exit fee would be \$400,000.

**Current All-In Interest Rate.** As of December 31, 2024, the all-in interest rate was approximately SOFR (4.33% as of such date) plus 3.25%, or 7.58% per annum. For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, total interest expense attributable to the term loan was approximately \$3.1 million, consistent with the Company\'s audited financial statements.

**[Section 3: Repayment and Amortization]{.underline}**

**Amortization.** The term loan amortizes in quarterly installments equal to 1.25% of the original drawn amount per quarter --- that is, \$500,000 per quarter based on the \$40.0 million drawn --- with the remaining outstanding principal balance due in a single bullet payment at maturity.

**Bullet Payment at Maturity.** All remaining unpaid principal, accrued and unpaid interest, the exit fee described in Section 2, and any other amounts owed under the Credit Agreement are due and payable on December 15, 2027.

**Mandatory Prepayments.** The Credit Agreement requires mandatory prepayments under the following circumstances:

> **(a) Asset Dispositions.** 100% of net cash proceeds from any asset disposition (other than dispositions of obsolete or worn-out equipment in the ordinary course of business) exceeding \$1.0 million in any fiscal year, subject to a reinvestment right permitting the Borrower to reinvest such proceeds within 180 days of receipt.
>
> **(b) Excess Cash Flow Sweep.** 50% of Excess Cash Flow (defined as Adjusted EBITDA minus capital expenditures, minus scheduled debt service, minus taxes paid in cash) for any fiscal year in which the Leverage Ratio (as defined in Section 6 below) exceeds 1.5x, payable within 90 days of fiscal year-end.
>
> **(c) Debt Issuance Proceeds.** 100% of net cash proceeds from any issuance of indebtedness (other than permitted indebtedness as defined in the Credit Agreement).
>
> **(d) Equity Issuance Proceeds --- Not Subject to Mandatory Prepayment.** Proceeds of an equity issuance, including proceeds of an initial public offering, are expressly excluded from the mandatory prepayment provisions. The IPO does not trigger a mandatory prepayment obligation.

**Voluntary Prepayment.** The Borrower may voluntarily prepay the term loan at any time, in whole or in part, in minimum increments of \$1.0 million, upon not less than 5 business days\' prior written notice to the Lender. If the term loan is repaid in full (whether voluntarily or at maturity), the exit fee described in Section 2 is payable.

**[Section 4: Security and Guarantees]{.underline}**

**Collateral.** The Borrower\'s obligations under the Credit Agreement are secured by a first-priority perfected security interest in substantially all assets of the Borrower, including without limitation:

> • All accounts receivable;
>
> • All intellectual property, including patents, trademarks, copyrights, and trade secrets --- specifically including the 47 issued U.S. patents, 12 pending U.S. patent applications, 8 foreign patents, and the registered trademarks \"Crestline,\" \"Crestline Nexus,\" and \"SupplyMind\";
>
> • All equipment and fixtures;
>
> • All deposit accounts (subject to deposit account control agreements as described below);
>
> • All equity interests in subsidiaries, limited to 65% of the voting equity interests of foreign subsidiaries (i.e., 65% of the voting equity of Crestline Software UK Ltd.);
>
> • All general intangibles, chattel paper, instruments, and investment property.

**Excluded Assets.** The following assets are excluded from the collateral package: real property leases (no mortgages have been granted), de minimis deposit accounts holding less than \$50,000, and any assets where the cost of perfection would be excessive relative to the value of the security interest obtained.

**Guarantee.** Crestline Software UK Ltd. provides a limited guarantee of the Borrower\'s obligations under the Credit Agreement, capped at £5,000,000, secured by a first-priority lien on the assets of Crestline Software UK Ltd. (subject to limitations under English law).

**Deposit Account Control Agreements.** The Borrower is required to maintain deposit account control agreements with respect to all U.S. deposit accounts holding more than \$250,000.

**[Section 5: Change of Control Provision]{.underline}**

**This section addresses a provision of critical importance to the Company\'s contemplated IPO.**

**5.1 Definition of \"Change of Control\"**

The Credit Agreement defines \"Change of Control\" as the occurrence of any of the following:

> **(a)** Any \"person\" or \"group\" (as such terms are used in Sections 13(d) and 14(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) that did not hold equity interests in the Borrower as of the closing date of the Credit Agreement acquires beneficial ownership of more than 50% of the voting power of the Borrower\'s then-outstanding capital stock;
>
> **(b)** The existing stockholders of the Borrower as of the closing date (collectively, the \"Existing Holders\") cease to hold, directly or indirectly, at least 50% of the aggregate voting power of the Borrower\'s then-outstanding capital stock, **including following an initial public offering**;
>
> **(c)** A merger, consolidation, or sale of all or substantially all assets of the Borrower, other than a merger or consolidation in which the Existing Holders retain at least 50% of the voting power of the surviving entity; or
>
> **(d)** Arun Ramaswamy ceases to serve as Chief Executive Officer of the Borrower, unless a successor reasonably acceptable to the Lender is appointed within 90 days.

**5.2 Consequence of Change of Control**

Upon a Change of Control, the Lender may, at its option, declare all outstanding principal, accrued and unpaid interest, the exit fee, and all other obligations under the Credit Agreement immediately due and payable. The Borrower is required to provide written notice to the Lender within 5 business days of becoming aware of a Change of Control event.

**5.3 IPO Applicability Analysis (Internal Reference)**

The following analysis evaluates whether the Company\'s contemplated IPO would trigger a Change of Control under the Credit Agreement:

**Clause (b) --- Voting Power Threshold.** This is the clause most relevant to an IPO. The key question is whether the Existing Holders will retain at least 50% of aggregate voting power following the offering. The Company\'s proposed amended and restated certificate of incorporation establishes a dual-class capital structure: Class A common stock carrying 1 vote per share and Class B common stock carrying 10 votes per share. Post-IPO, Co-Founders Arun Ramaswamy (12,200,000 Class B shares) and Katerina \"Kate\" Litvak (8,600,000 Class B shares) will hold an aggregate of 20,800,000 Class B shares. The voting power calculation is as follows:

> • Class B votes: 20,800,000 × 10 = 208,000,000
>
> • Class A votes: 81,616,667 × 1 = 81,616,667
>
> • Total votes: 289,616,667
>
> • Class B (Existing Holder) percentage: 208,000,000 / 289,616,667 = **71.8%**

Accordingly, the Existing Holders will retain approximately **71.8%** of aggregate voting power, which exceeds the 50% threshold. **The Change of Control provision under clause (b) would NOT be triggered by the contemplated IPO.**

**Clause (d) --- Key Person.** Arun Ramaswamy will continue to serve as Chief Executive Officer. **Not triggered.**

**Clauses (a) and (c).** The IPO involves a primary issuance of equity securities; it does not involve an acquisition by a new \"person\" or \"group\" of more than 50% of voting power, nor is it a merger, consolidation, or asset sale. **Not triggered.**

**Conclusion: The IPO as currently structured does not constitute a Change of Control under the Credit Agreement.**

**5.4 Pre-IPO Notification Requirement --- Section 7.14 (Critical)**

Independent of the Change of Control provision, Section 7.14 of the Credit Agreement (titled \"Public Offering Notice\") contains a separate, standalone affirmative covenant: **The Borrower shall provide the Lender with written notice not less than thirty (30) days prior to the consummation of any initial public offering of the Borrower\'s equity securities** (an \"IPO Notice\"). The IPO Notice must include:

> \(i\) the anticipated date of such offering;
>
> \(ii\) the anticipated gross proceeds;
>
> \(iii\) the anticipated use of proceeds, including whether any proceeds will be used to prepay the term loan; and
>
> \(iv\) a pro forma capitalization table reflecting the offering.

The 30-day IPO Notice requirement is **independent** of and **in addition to** the Change of Control provision. It is a standalone affirmative covenant under the Credit Agreement. Failure to deliver the IPO Notice within the required timeframe constitutes an Event of Default under Section 8.1(c) (failure to comply with affirmative covenants), subject to a 10-business-day cure period following written notice from the Lender.

**Note for counsel:** This notification requirement must be satisfied well in advance of pricing. The Company\'s outside counsel (Thorncroft & Wexler LLP) and CFO Michael D. Hartwell should coordinate timely preparation and delivery of the IPO Notice to Kestridge West Credit Partners. The S-1 Registration Statement should disclose this notification requirement and the Lender\'s potential remedies if notice is not timely provided. The 30-day notice period must be factored into the IPO timeline.

**[Section 6: Financial Covenants]{.underline}**

**Minimum Liquidity Covenant.** The Borrower shall maintain at all times unrestricted cash, cash equivalents, and eligible short-term investments (collectively, \"Liquidity\") of not less than **\$30,000,000**.

> *As of December 31, 2024:* Cash and cash equivalents = \$94.7 million; short-term investments = \$42.3 million; Total Liquidity = \$137.0 million. **Covenant is satisfied with substantial headroom.**

**ARR-Based Leverage Ratio Covenant.** As of the last day of each fiscal quarter, the ratio of (a) Total Funded Debt to (b) the Borrower\'s annualized recurring revenue (\"ARR\") as of such date shall not exceed **2.0x**.

> \"Total Funded Debt\" means all outstanding indebtedness for borrowed money, including the term loan, capital leases, and any drawn amounts under revolving credit facilities. As of December 31, 2024: term loan outstanding = \$40.0 million; no capital leases or revolving facility draws; Total Funded Debt = \$40.0 million. ARR as of December 31, 2024 = \$211.6 million. Leverage Ratio = \$40.0M / \$211.6M = **0.19x**. **Covenant is satisfied with substantial headroom** (maximum permitted: 2.0x).

**Testing Frequency.** Both financial covenants are tested quarterly, as of the last day of each fiscal quarter, with compliance certificates due within 45 days of quarter-end.

**Financial Reporting Covenants.** The Borrower shall deliver to the Lender: (a) audited annual financial statements within 90 days of fiscal year-end; (b) unaudited quarterly financial statements within 45 days of quarter-end; (c) a compliance certificate signed by the CFO accompanying each set of financial statements; and (d) prompt notice of any material litigation or material adverse change.

**[Section 7: Affirmative and Negative Covenants (Summary)]{.underline}**

**7.1 Key Affirmative Covenants**

In addition to the financial covenants described in Section 6 and the IPO Notice requirement in Section 7.14 of the Credit Agreement (described in Section 5.4 above), the principal affirmative covenants include:

> • Maintenance of corporate existence and good standing in all jurisdictions where the Company conducts business;
>
> • Maintenance of insurance coverage, including directors\' and officers\' liability, cyber liability, and commercial general liability;
>
> • Compliance with all material laws and regulations, including the General Data Protection Regulation as applicable to Crestline Software UK Ltd.;
>
> • Payment of taxes when due;
>
> • Maintenance and protection of intellectual property rights;
>
> • Provision of access to books, records, and properties upon reasonable notice from the Lender;
>
> • Notification to the Lender of any Event of Default within 3 business days of discovery; and
>
> • Notification to the Lender of any material litigation, including the pending action captioned *Optivance, Inc. v. Crestline Software, Inc.*, Case No. 6:24-cv-00831 (E.D. Tex.), filed September 12, 2024.

**7.2 Key Negative Covenants**

> **Indebtedness.** No additional indebtedness exceeding \$5.0 million in aggregate without Lender consent, with carve-outs for capital leases up to \$3.0 million, purchase money indebtedness, and intercompany loans to subsidiaries.
>
> **Liens.** No additional liens on collateral other than permitted liens (statutory tax liens, purchase money liens, and immaterial liens not exceeding \$500,000 in aggregate).
>
> **Investments.** No investments exceeding \$2.0 million individually or \$10.0 million in aggregate, with carve-outs for short-term marketable securities, intercompany investments, and the Company\'s existing equity investment portfolio.
>
> **Restricted Payments.** No dividends or other distributions on equity interests, and no stock repurchases, without Lender consent; *provided* that the Borrower may repurchase equity from departing employees at fair market value in an amount not to exceed \$2.0 million per fiscal year.
>
> **Mergers and Acquisitions.** No mergers, consolidations, or asset sales without Lender consent, other than dispositions of obsolete assets in the ordinary course of business up to \$1.0 million per fiscal year.
>
> **Affiliate Transactions.** All transactions with affiliates must be on arm\'s-length terms and, if exceeding \$500,000 in value, require prior written notification to the Lender. (Note: The Company\'s SaaS agreement with Vantara Logistics, Inc. at \$1.2 million annually would require notification under this covenant.)

**7.3 Amendment or Waiver**

Any amendment to, or waiver of, any covenant under the Credit Agreement requires the prior written consent of the Lender.

**[Section 8: Events of Default]{.underline}**

The Credit Agreement defines the following Events of Default (among others):

> **(a)** Failure to pay principal, interest, or fees when due (subject to a 3-business-day cure period for interest and fees only);
>
> **(b)** Breach of any financial covenant (no cure period);
>
> **(c)** Failure to comply with any affirmative or negative covenant (subject to a 10-business-day cure period for affirmative covenants other than financial covenants and payment obligations; no cure period for negative covenants);
>
> **(d)** Material inaccuracy of any representation or warranty at the time made;
>
> **(e)** Cross-default: default under any other indebtedness of the Borrower exceeding \$2.0 million;
>
> **(f)** Bankruptcy, insolvency, or similar proceedings involving the Borrower or any subsidiary;
>
> **(g)** Change of Control (as defined in Section 5 above);
>
> **(h)** One or more judgments for the payment of money in excess of \$5.0 million in the aggregate that remain undischarged for 60 days (relevant to the pending Optivance litigation, where management\'s outside patent counsel estimates potential exposure of \$5 million to \$20 million --- an adverse judgment at the upper end could trigger this Event of Default if not bonded, appealed, or otherwise stayed); and
>
> **(i)** Material adverse change in the financial condition, business, operations, or prospects of the Borrower.

**Remedies upon Event of Default.** Upon the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default, the Lender may, at its option: (i) accelerate all outstanding obligations and declare them immediately due and payable; (ii) terminate any remaining commitment; (iii) exercise remedies under the security agreement, including foreclosure on the collateral; and (iv) pursue all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.

**Waiver.** No waiver of any Event of Default shall be effective unless set forth in a written instrument signed by the Lender.

**Note:** Failure to deliver the 30-day IPO Notice required by Section 7.14 constitutes a breach of an affirmative covenant, giving rise to an Event of Default under clause (c) above, subject to the 10-business-day cure period.

**[Section 9: Representations and Warranties (Summary)]{.underline}**

The Credit Agreement contains standard representations and warranties made by the Borrower as of the closing date and deemed repeated on each draw date, including without limitation:

> • Valid organization and good standing in Delaware and all jurisdictions where the Company conducts business;
>
> • Due authorization and enforceability of the Credit Agreement and all ancillary documents;
>
> • No violation of organizational documents, material agreements, or applicable law;
>
> • Accuracy and completeness of financial statements delivered to the Lender (most recent: audited financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, prepared in accordance with GAAP and audited by Archer Pryce LLP);
>
> • No material adverse change since the date of the most recent financial statements;
>
> • Clear title to collateral, free of all liens other than permitted liens;
>
> • Ownership and validity of intellectual property, with no knowledge of material infringement (subject to required update regarding the Optivance litigation filed post-closing);
>
> • Compliance with ERISA and tax obligations;
>
> • Accuracy of beneficial ownership certification under the Customer Identification Program and the Corporate Transparency Act; and
>
> • No pending or threatened litigation that would reasonably be expected to result in a material adverse effect (subject to required update regarding Optivance).

**Bring-Down Representation.** Each delivery of a compliance certificate constitutes a reaffirmation of all representations and warranties as of such date, except to the extent previously disclosed to the Lender in writing.

**[Section 10: Conditions Precedent to Funding]{.underline}**

**Initial Draw Conditions** (satisfied at closing on December 15, 2022):

> • Execution and delivery of the Credit Agreement, security agreement, and all ancillary documents;
>
> • Perfection of security interests (UCC-1 filings, deposit account control agreements, intellectual property security agreement filings with the USPTO);
>
> • Delivery of legal opinions from the Borrower\'s counsel, Thorncroft & Wexler LLP, 600 Lexington Avenue, 38th Floor, New York, NY 10022;
>
> • Delivery of audited financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2021;
>
> • Delivery of a compliance certificate and solvency certificate signed by the CFO, Michael D. Hartwell;
>
> • Payment of the upfront fee (\$250,000) and reimbursement of the Lender\'s legal expenses; and
>
> • No Event of Default or material adverse change.

**Subsequent Draw Conditions** (availability period expired June 30, 2024; no further draws available):

> • Bring-down of representations and warranties;
>
> • No Event of Default; and
>
> • Delivery of a borrowing request with not less than 3 business days\' prior notice.

**[Section 11: Miscellaneous Provisions]{.underline}**

**Governing Law.** The Credit Agreement is governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflict of laws principles.

**Jurisdiction.** The parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of New York located in the Borough of Manhattan, New York County, and the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York.

**Waiver of Jury Trial.** Both parties irrevocably waive the right to trial by jury in any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to the Credit Agreement.

**Assignments.** The Lender may assign its rights and obligations under the Credit Agreement with the consent of the Borrower (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld), except that no consent of the Borrower is required during the continuance of an Event of Default.

**Notices.** All notices must be in writing and delivered to the following addresses:

> *To the Borrower:* Crestline Software, Inc., 4200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759, Attention: Sandra Y. Chen, General Counsel.
>
> *To the Lender:* Kestridge West Credit Partners, at the address specified in the Credit Agreement.

**Confidentiality.** The Lender agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the Borrower\'s proprietary information, subject to standard carve-outs for regulatory requirements, legal process, and disclosures to the Lender\'s affiliates and advisors bound by confidentiality obligations.

**Amendments.** No amendment to, or waiver of any provision of, the Credit Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.

**Expenses.** The Borrower is responsible for all reasonable and documented costs and expenses incurred by the Lender in connection with the preparation, administration, and enforcement of the Credit Agreement, including reasonable legal fees.

**[Section 12: Summary Table of Key Terms]{.underline}**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Term**                            **Detail**
  ----------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------
  Borrower                            Crestline Software, Inc.

  Lender                              Kestridge West Credit Partners

  Facility Type                       Senior Secured Term Loan

  Commitment Amount                   \$50,000,000

  Amount Drawn                        \$40,000,000 (as of December 31, 2024)

  Interest Rate                       SOFR + 3.25% (SOFR floor: 1.00%)

  Maturity Date                       December 15, 2027

  Amortization                        1.25% of drawn amount per quarter (\$500,000/quarter)

  Minimum Liquidity Covenant          \$30,000,000

  ARR Leverage Ratio Covenant         Total Funded Debt / ARR ≤ 2.0x

  Current Leverage Ratio              0.19x (\$40.0M / \$211.6M ARR)

  Current Liquidity                   \$137.0M (\$94.7M cash + \$42.3M short-term investments)

  Change of Control Trigger           Existing Holders \< 50% voting power

  IPO Notice Requirement              **30 days\' prior written notice before any IPO (Section 7.14)**

  Collateral                          Substantially all assets (first-priority lien)

  Guarantor                           Crestline Software UK Ltd. (limited guarantee, £5M cap)

  Exit Fee                            1.00% of outstanding principal at repayment

  Governing Law                       New York
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 13: IPO Considerations and Action Items]{.underline}**

*This section serves as an internal counsel\'s note summarizing the Credit Agreement implications for the planned IPO and is intended for the working group comprising Sandra Y. Chen (General Counsel), Michael D. Hartwell (CFO), Thorncroft & Wexler LLP (outside counsel), and Archer Pryce LLP (auditors).*

**(1) Change of Control --- No Trigger Expected.** Based on the proposed dual-class capital structure (Class A: 1 vote per share; Class B: 10 votes per share), the Co-Founders will hold approximately 71.8% of aggregate voting power post-IPO. This exceeds the 50% Existing Holder threshold in the Change of Control definition under Section 5.1(b) of the Credit Agreement. No acceleration event is anticipated under any clause of the Change of Control provision.

**(2) 30-Day IPO Notice --- ACTION REQUIRED.** Section 7.14 of the Credit Agreement requires delivery of a written IPO Notice to Kestridge West Credit Partners not less than 30 days prior to the consummation of the IPO. The IPO Notice must include: (i) the anticipated offering date, (ii) the anticipated gross proceeds, (iii) the planned use of proceeds (including whether any proceeds will be used to prepay the term loan), and (iv) a pro forma capitalization table reflecting the offering. Failure to deliver this notice constitutes an Event of Default under Section 8.1(c), subject to a 10-business-day cure period following written notice from the Lender. **Recommendation:** Sandra Y. Chen and Michael D. Hartwell should coordinate preparation and delivery of the IPO Notice at least 35 days prior to the expected pricing date to allow for delivery logistics and any clarifications requested by the Lender. Thorncroft & Wexler LLP should review the IPO Notice before delivery.

**(3) Restricted Payments Covenant.** The negative covenant on restricted payments prohibits dividends and stock repurchases without Lender consent. This is consistent with the Company\'s planned dividend policy, under which no dividends are anticipated for the foreseeable future. Confirm appropriate disclosure in the S-1 \"Dividend Policy\" section.

**(4) Mandatory Prepayment --- Not Triggered by IPO.** IPO proceeds (equity issuance) are explicitly excluded from the mandatory prepayment sweep provisions of the Credit Agreement. The Company does not intend to use IPO proceeds to repay the term loan. Confirm disclosure in the S-1 \"Use of Proceeds\" section.

**(5) Disclosure in S-1.** The term loan, including its material terms, financial covenants, collateral, Change of Control provision, and the 30-day IPO Notice requirement under Section 7.14, must be disclosed in the S-1 Registration Statement under \"Description of Indebtedness\" and summarized as applicable in \"Use of Proceeds\" and \"Risk Factors.\"

**(6) Optivance Litigation --- Judgment Threshold.** Under Section 8.1(h) of the Credit Agreement, one or more judgments for the payment of money in excess of \$5.0 million in the aggregate that remain undischarged for 60 days constitute an Event of Default. Outside patent counsel currently estimates potential exposure in the Optivance matter at \$5 million to \$20 million. While counsel assesses the probability of an adverse outcome as low, this threshold should be actively monitored, and the working group should assess bonding, appeal, or stay strategies in the event of an unfavorable result.

*This Executive Summary was prepared by Sandra Y. Chen, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, Crestline Software, Inc., for the sole benefit of management and outside counsel in connection with the Company\'s contemplated initial public offering. This document is privileged and confidential and is not intended for distribution to any third party without the prior written consent of the General Counsel.*

Attn: Michael D. Hartwell, Chief Financial Officer, and Sandra Y. Chen, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary

**From:** David R. Calloway, Managing Director Pinnacle Ridge Capital 200 Park Avenue New York, NY 10166

This term sheet (this \"Term Sheet\") sets forth certain proposed terms and conditions for the initial public offering (the \"Offering\") of shares of Class A common stock, par value \$0.0001 per share (the \"Shares\"), of Crestline Software, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Company\"). This Term Sheet is for discussion purposes only, is not binding, and is subject to the execution and delivery of a definitive underwriting agreement (the \"Underwriting Agreement\").

**cc:** Sarah K. Norden, Thorncroft & Wexler LLP (Issuer\'s Counsel); Whitaker & Simms LLP (Underwriters\' Counsel)

**[Section 1: Offering Summary]{.underline}**

**Issuer:** Crestline Software, Inc., a Delaware C-corporation; CIK 0002014387; EIN 84-3291074.

**Securities:** 15,000,000 shares of Class A common stock, par value \$0.0001 per share. All primary shares; no secondary sales.

**Proposed Exchange Listing:** Nasdaq Global Select Market.

**Proposed Ticker Symbol:** CRST.

**Type of Offering:** Firm commitment underwritten initial public offering pursuant to a Registration Statement on Form S-1 to be filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"SEC\").

**Lead Left Book-Runner:** Pinnacle Ridge Capital, 200 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10166.

**Co-Book-Runner:** Stonebridge Greer & Co., 555 California Street, Suite 4200, San Francisco, CA 94104.

**Issuer\'s Counsel:** Thorncroft & Wexler LLP, 600 Lexington Avenue, 38th Floor, New York, NY 10022 (Lead Partner: Sarah K. Norden; Associate: James T. Okafor).

**Underwriters\' Counsel:** Whitaker & Simms LLP, 51 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019.

**Independent Auditor:** Archer Pryce LLP, One PPG Place, Suite 2200, Pittsburgh, PA 15222 (Engagement Partner: Robert C. Fenn).

**Transfer Agent:** Oakvale Transfer Transfer Co.

**Dual-Class Structure:** The Company will have two classes of common stock: Class A common stock (1 vote per share; offered to the public) and Class B common stock (10 votes per share; held by co-founders Arun Ramaswamy and Katerina \"Kate\" Litvak). All outstanding preferred stock converts into common stock upon the closing of the Offering. Class B share holdings: Ramaswamy --- 12,200,000 shares; Litvak --- 8,600,000 shares; Total Class B --- 20,800,000 shares.

**[Section 2: Pricing and Economics]{.underline}**

**Expected Price Range:** \$22.00 to \$26.00 per share (midpoint: \$24.00 per share).

**Base Offering Size:** 15,000,000 shares of Class A common stock (all primary; no secondary offering).

**Gross Proceeds (at midpoint):** 15,000,000 × \$24.00 = \$360,000,000.

**Underwriting Discount:** 6.5% of gross proceeds; at midpoint = \$23,400,000.

> • Management fee: 20% (\$4,680,000)
>
> • Underwriting fee: 20% (\$4,680,000)
>
> • Selling concession: 60% (\$14,040,000)
>
> • Split between underwriters: Pinnacle Ridge Capital (lead left): \[●\]%; Stonebridge Greer & Co.: \[●\]% (to be agreed)

**Net Proceeds to Company (before expenses, at midpoint):** \$360,000,000 − \$23,400,000 = \$336,600,000.

**Estimated Offering Expenses (excluding underwriting discount):** \$4,800,000 (including SEC registration fee, FINRA filing fee, Nasdaq listing fee, printing, legal, accounting, transfer agent, and miscellaneous expenses).

**Net Proceeds to Company (after all costs, at midpoint):** \$336,600,000 − \$4,800,000 = \$331,800,000.

**Over-Allotment Option:** The Underwriters shall have a 30-day option to purchase up to 2,250,000 additional shares of Class A common stock (15% of the base offering) from the Company at the initial public offering price, less the underwriting discount, solely to cover over-allotments.

**Shares Outstanding Post-Offering (base, no over-allotment exercise):** 52,000,000 (pre-IPO common) + 35,416,667 (preferred converting 1:1) + 15,000,000 (new shares) = 102,416,667 shares total; Class A: 81,616,667; Class B: 20,800,000.

**Shares Outstanding Post-Offering (with full over-allotment):** 102,416,667 + 2,250,000 = 104,666,667 shares total.

  **Scenario**    **Price**   **Gross Proceeds**   **Discount (6.5%)**   **Net Before Expenses**   **Net After Expenses**
  --------------- ----------- -------------------- --------------------- ------------------------- ------------------------
  Low (no OA)     \$22.00     \$330.0M             \$21.45M              \$308.55M                 \$303.75M

  Mid (no OA)     \$24.00     \$360.0M             \$23.40M              \$336.60M                 \$331.80M

  High (no OA)    \$26.00     \$390.0M             \$25.35M              \$364.65M                 \$359.85M

  Mid (full OA)   \$24.00     \$414.0M             \$26.91M              \$387.09M                 \$382.29M
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 3: Capitalization Overview]{.underline}**

**Pre-IPO Capitalization:**

> • *Common stock outstanding:* 52,000,000 shares
>
> • *Redeemable convertible preferred stock:* 35,416,667 shares total
>
> • Series A: 8,000,000 shares
>
> • Series B: 9,500,000 shares
>
> • Series C: 7,916,667 shares
>
> • Series D: 10,000,000 shares
>
> • Aggregate liquidation preference: \$385.0 million
>
> • All preferred shares convert 1:1 into common stock upon closing of the Offering
>
> • *Stock options outstanding:* 14,750,000 (weighted average exercise price: \$8.42/share)
>
> • *RSUs outstanding:* 3,200,000
>
> • *Fully diluted shares pre-IPO (before offering):* 52,000,000 + 35,416,667 + 14,750,000 + 3,200,000 = 105,366,667
>
> • *2024 Equity Incentive Plan:* 18,500,000 shares authorized; 17,950,000 issued/outstanding; 550,000 remaining
>
> • *2024 ESPP:* 2,500,000 shares reserved; to become effective upon the closing of the Offering

The capitalization table in the S-1 Registration Statement will present actual, pro forma (giving effect to preferred conversion), and pro forma as adjusted (giving effect to preferred conversion and the Offering) columns. Following the Offering, the co-founders will hold approximately 71.8% of the combined voting power through their Class B common stock holdings.

**[Section 4: Use of Proceeds]{.underline}**

The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the Offering for general corporate purposes, including working capital, research and development, sales and marketing, and potential strategic acquisitions or investments. A portion of net proceeds may be used to repay indebtedness under the Company\'s existing \$50.0 million term loan facility with Kestridge West Credit Partners (\$40.0 million currently outstanding, maturing December 2027, bearing interest at SOFR + 3.25%). The term loan agreement requires 30-day prior written notice to the lender before any public offering. The Company has not determined the specific allocation of net proceeds among these uses, and the amounts and timing may vary.

**[Section 5: Lock-Up Arrangements]{.underline}**

**5.1 Lock-Up Terms Overview**

**Lock-Up Period:** 180 days following the date of the final prospectus forming a part of the Registration Statement (the \"Lock-Up Period\").

**Restricted Persons:** Each of the following persons (each, a \"Lock-Up Party\") shall execute and deliver a Lock-Up Agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A:

> \(a\) All executive officers of the Company, including Arun Ramaswamy (CEO), Katerina \"Kate\" Litvak (CTO), Michael D. Hartwell (CFO), and Sandra Y. Chen (General Counsel);
>
> \(b\) All members of the Board of Directors of the Company, including Priya Venkatesh, Thomas J. Moreno, Dr. Helen Park, James Kwon, and Adaora Obi; and
>
> \(c\) All stockholders of the Company beneficially owning 5% or more of the Company\'s outstanding capital stock immediately prior to the Offering.

The Lock-Up Agreements shall be executed by each director listed in clause (b) above in their individual capacity. The Company shall also execute a company lock-up restricting the issuance of new shares during the Lock-Up Period, subject to customary exceptions for equity incentive plan grants and other ordinary course issuances.

**5.2 Lock-Up Restrictions**

During the Lock-Up Period, no Lock-Up Party shall, without the prior written consent of Pinnacle Ridge Capital (which consent may be withheld in its sole discretion):

> \(i\) offer, sell, contract to sell, pledge, grant any option to purchase, make any short sale, or otherwise dispose of, directly or indirectly, any shares of capital stock of the Company or any securities convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for shares of capital stock of the Company;
>
> \(ii\) enter into any swap, hedge, or other arrangement that transfers, in whole or in part, any of the economic consequences of ownership of shares of the Company\'s capital stock;
>
> \(iii\) make any demand for, or exercise any right with respect to, the registration of any shares of the Company\'s capital stock under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"Securities Act\"); or
>
> \(iv\) publicly disclose the intention to do any of the foregoing.

These restrictions apply to shares currently held, shares acquired during the Lock-Up Period, and shares issuable upon exercise of options, conversion of preferred stock, or vesting of RSUs.

**5.3 Customary Exceptions to Lock-Up**

The Lock-Up Agreement shall include customary exceptions permitting:

> \(a\) transfers as a bona fide gift or gifts, provided the donee executes a lock-up agreement;
>
> \(b\) transfers to a trust for the direct or indirect benefit of the Lock-Up Party or their immediate family, provided the trustee executes a lock-up agreement;
>
> \(c\) transfers by operation of law, including pursuant to a domestic relations order;
>
> \(d\) transfers to affiliates or members/partners of a Lock-Up Party that is a partnership, limited liability company, or corporation, provided each transferee executes a lock-up agreement;
>
> \(e\) dispositions to the Company in connection with the exercise of stock options on a \"net exercise\" or \"cashless exercise\" basis, or for the payment of tax withholding obligations in connection with the vesting of RSUs, provided the underlying shares remain subject to the lock-up;
>
> \(f\) sales pursuant to a trading plan established under Rule 10b5-1 prior to the date of the Lock-Up Agreement, provided no public filing or announcement is required or made during the Lock-Up Period;
>
> \(g\) establishment of a new trading plan under Rule 10b5-1 during the Lock-Up Period, provided no sales occur thereunder during the Lock-Up Period and no public announcement is required;
>
> \(h\) transfers pursuant to a bona fide third-party tender offer, merger, consolidation, or similar transaction involving a change of control of the Company approved by the Board, provided that if such transaction is not consummated, the shares remain subject to the lock-up; and
>
> \(i\) sales of shares acquired in the open market following the closing of the Offering.

Each exception requires that no public filing under Section 16(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, is voluntarily made during the Lock-Up Period in connection with such transfer (except as required by law and except for Form 5 filings).

**5.4 Early Release and Waiver Provisions**

Pinnacle Ridge Capital may, in its sole discretion, release any Lock-Up Party from the restrictions of the Lock-Up Agreement at any time prior to the expiration of the Lock-Up Period. If Pinnacle Ridge Capital releases any officer or director from the lock-up restrictions, or waives the lock-up restrictions with respect to any shares representing more than 1% of the Company\'s outstanding shares, Pinnacle Ridge Capital shall provide the Company with at least three (3) business days\' prior notice of such release or waiver. The Company shall announce any such release or waiver by press release through a major news service at least two (2) business days before the effective date of any such release or waiver. The lock-up restrictions shall terminate automatically upon the earlier of (a) the expiration of the 180-day Lock-Up Period or (b) the date on which Pinnacle Ridge Capital provides written notice to the Company that the lock-up restrictions are terminated.

**[Section 6: Conditions to Underwriting]{.underline}**

The obligations of the Underwriters under the Underwriting Agreement will be subject to customary conditions, including:

> \(a\) Effectiveness of the Registration Statement on Form S-1 and no stop order issued or proceedings therefor initiated or threatened by the SEC;
>
> \(b\) Receipt of comfort letters from Archer Pryce LLP (independent auditor);
>
> \(c\) Receipt of opinions and negative assurance letters from Thorncroft & Wexler LLP (Issuer\'s counsel) and Whitaker & Simms LLP (Underwriters\' counsel);
>
> \(d\) Receipt of officer\'s certificates from the CEO and CFO;
>
> \(e\) Delivery of executed Lock-Up Agreements from all persons identified in Section 5.1;
>
> \(f\) No material adverse change in the Company\'s business, financial condition, or results of operations;
>
> \(g\) Listing approval from the Nasdaq Global Select Market;
>
> \(h\) Receipt of all necessary regulatory approvals;
>
> \(i\) Absence of any pending or threatened litigation that could reasonably be expected to materially and adversely affect the Offering (note: the pending *Optivance, Inc. v. Crestline Software, Inc.* patent infringement litigation (Case No. 6:24-cv-00831, E.D. Tex.) has been disclosed to the Underwriters and is not expected to constitute a material adverse effect); and
>
> \(j\) Delivery of lock-up agreements from all officers, directors, and 5%+ stockholders as described in Section 5.

**[Section 7: Representations and Warranties of the Company]{.underline}**

The Underwriting Agreement will contain customary representations and warranties by the Company, including as to:

> \(a\) due organization and good standing; (b) authorization and validity of the Shares; (c) accuracy of the Registration Statement and prospectus (no untrue statement of material fact or omission); (d) financial statements prepared in accordance with GAAP and audited by Archer Pryce LLP; (e) absence of undisclosed liabilities; (f) no material adverse change since December 31, 2024; (g) compliance with applicable laws and regulations; (h) good title to all properties and assets; (i) validity and enforceability of all material contracts; (j) intellectual property rights (47 issued U.S. patents; 12 pending U.S. applications; 8 issued foreign patents); (k) tax compliance; (l) no undisclosed related party transactions; (m) proper classification of equity and debt on the Company\'s balance sheet; (n) compliance with data privacy laws (GDPR, CCPA); and (o) no undisclosed pending or threatened litigation other than as disclosed in the Registration Statement.

**[Section 8: Indemnification and Contribution]{.underline}**

The Underwriting Agreement will contain customary indemnification provisions:

**Company Indemnification of Underwriters.** The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter against losses, claims, damages, liabilities, and expenses arising out of any untrue statement or omission of material fact in the Registration Statement, prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, except for information furnished in writing by the Underwriters for inclusion therein.

**Underwriter Indemnification of Company.** Each Underwriter shall indemnify the Company against losses arising from untrue statements or omissions based on information furnished in writing by such Underwriter for inclusion in the Registration Statement or prospectus.

**Contribution.** If indemnification is unavailable, each party shall contribute to losses in proportion to the relative benefits received (underwriting discount vs. net proceeds) and relative fault.

**[Section 9: Stabilization, Short Positions, and Penalty Bids]{.underline}**

Pinnacle Ridge Capital, as lead book-runner, may engage in the following activities in connection with the Offering:

**Stabilization.** The Underwriters may bid for or purchase shares of Class A common stock in the open market to stabilize the price, subject to Regulation M under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

**Short Sales and Over-Allotment.** The Underwriters may sell more shares than the base offering size, creating a short position of up to 2,250,000 shares. The Underwriters may close any short position by exercising the over-allotment option or purchasing shares in the open market.

**Penalty Bids.** Pinnacle Ridge Capital may impose a penalty bid, whereby selling concessions allowed to a syndicate member may be reclaimed if shares sold by such member are repurchased in stabilizing or short-covering transactions.

These activities may have the effect of raising or maintaining the market price of the Shares above independent market levels or preventing or retarding a decline in market price.

**[Section 10: Other Terms]{.underline}**

**Governing Law.** The Underwriting Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

**Expenses.** The Company shall bear all offering expenses (estimated at \$4,800,000 excluding the underwriting discount), including SEC registration fees, FINRA filing fees, Nasdaq listing fees, printing, legal, and accounting expenses. The Underwriters shall bear their own internal costs and the fees of Whitaker & Simms LLP.

**Blue Sky.** The Company shall cooperate with the Underwriters in qualifying the Shares for sale under applicable state securities laws.

**Emerging Growth Company Status.** The Company represents that it qualifies as an \"emerging growth company\" as defined in Section 2(a)(19) of the Securities Act, as amended by the JOBS Act. The Company intends to avail itself of certain reduced disclosure and reporting requirements available to EGCs.

**FINRA.** The terms of the Offering shall comply with FINRA Rule 5110 regarding underwriting compensation.

**Effective Date of ESPP.** The Company\'s 2024 Employee Stock Purchase Plan (2,500,000 shares reserved) will become effective upon the closing of the Offering.

**Material Contracts Disclosure.** The Company shall ensure that the Registration Statement discloses all material agreements, including the term loan with Kestridge West Credit Partners and the Investor Rights Agreement dated June 2023 providing registration rights to Graymont Ventures, Ridgeline Growth Partners, and other preferred stockholders.

**Termination.** Pinnacle Ridge Capital may terminate the Underwriting Agreement at any time prior to the closing of the Offering upon the occurrence of certain events, including material adverse change, market disruption, or governmental action.

**[Section 11: Non-Binding Nature; Exclusivity]{.underline}**

This Term Sheet does not constitute a binding agreement and is subject in all respects to the negotiation, execution, and delivery of a definitive Underwriting Agreement. The Company agrees that during the period from the date of this Term Sheet until the earlier of (a) the execution of the Underwriting Agreement or (b) sixty (60) days from the date hereof, the Company shall not engage in discussions with any other underwriter or placement agent regarding the Offering without the prior written consent of Pinnacle Ridge Capital. All obligations of the parties are subject to the satisfactory completion of due diligence by the Underwriters and their counsel.

**PINNACLE RIDGE CAPITAL**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: David R. Calloway

Title: Managing Director

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

**STONEBRIDGE GREER & CO.**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Title: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

*Acknowledged and agreed (for purposes of the exclusivity provision in Section 11 only):*

**CRESTLINE SOFTWARE, INC.**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Michael D. Hartwell

Title: Chief Financial Officer

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

**EXHIBIT A**

**FORM OF LOCK-UP AGREEMENT**

Pinnacle Ridge Capital As Representative of the several Underwriters 200 Park Avenue New York, NY 10166

Ladies and Gentlemen:

The undersigned understands that Pinnacle Ridge Capital (the \"Representative\"), as representative of the several underwriters (the \"Underwriters\"), proposes to enter into an Underwriting Agreement (the \"Underwriting Agreement\") with Crestline Software, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"Company\"), providing for the public offering (the \"Public Offering\") of shares of Class A common stock, par value \$0.0001 per share, of the Company (the \"Securities\"). Capitalized terms used but not defined herein shall have the meanings set forth in the Underwriting Agreement.

**Section 1: Lock-Up Restriction**

In consideration of the agreement of the Underwriters to proceed with the Public Offering, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the undersigned hereby agrees that, during the period beginning on the date of the final prospectus relating to the Public Offering (the \"Prospectus\") and ending 180 days after the date of the Prospectus (the \"Lock-Up Period\"), the undersigned will not, without the prior written consent of the Representative:

> \(a\) offer, sell, contract to sell, pledge, grant any option, right or warrant to purchase, make any short sale, or otherwise transfer or dispose of, directly or indirectly, any shares of Common Stock of the Company or any securities convertible into, exercisable for, or exchangeable for shares of Common Stock of the Company that are beneficially owned (as such term is used in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the \"Exchange Act\")) by the undersigned on the date hereof, or that may be acquired by the undersigned hereafter (collectively, \"Lock-Up Securities\");
>
> \(b\) enter into any swap or other arrangement that transfers to another, in whole or in part, any of the economic consequences of ownership of any Lock-Up Securities, whether any such transaction described in clause (a) or (b) above is to be settled by delivery of Common Stock or other securities, in cash or otherwise;
>
> \(c\) make any demand for, or exercise any right with respect to, the registration of any shares of Common Stock or any security convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for Common Stock; or
>
> \(d\) publicly disclose the intention to do any of the foregoing.

This Lock-Up Agreement is being executed by the undersigned in the undersigned\'s individual capacity and relates to Lock-Up Securities beneficially owned by the undersigned.

**Section 2: Exceptions**

The restrictions set forth in Section 1 shall not apply to:

> \(a\) transactions relating to shares of Common Stock or other securities acquired in open market transactions after the completion of the Public Offering, provided that no filing under Section 16(a) of the Exchange Act shall be required or shall be voluntarily made in connection with subsequent sales of such securities during the Lock-Up Period;
>
> \(b\) bona fide gifts, provided that the donee shall sign and deliver a lock-up agreement substantially in the form of this Lock-Up Agreement;
>
> \(c\) transfers to any trust for the direct or indirect benefit of the undersigned or the immediate family of the undersigned (as defined below), provided that the trustee of the trust shall sign and deliver a lock-up agreement substantially in the form of this Lock-Up Agreement and provided further that any such transfer shall not involve a disposition for value;
>
> \(d\) distributions of Lock-Up Securities to limited partners, members, stockholders, or other equity holders of the undersigned, provided that each such distributee shall sign and deliver a lock-up agreement substantially in the form of this Lock-Up Agreement;
>
> \(e\) transfers by operation of law, such as pursuant to an order of a court or regulatory agency or pursuant to a domestic order or negotiated divorce settlement, provided that the transferee shall sign and deliver a lock-up agreement substantially in the form of this Lock-Up Agreement;
>
> \(f\) the exercise of stock options or the settlement of RSUs granted under the Company\'s equity incentive plans, provided that any shares of Common Stock received upon such exercise or settlement shall be subject to the terms of this Lock-Up Agreement, and provided further that any filing under Section 16(a) made in connection therewith shall clearly indicate in the footnotes thereto that the shares remain subject to the lock-up restrictions;
>
> \(g\) the establishment of a written trading plan pursuant to Rule 10b5-1 under the Exchange Act, provided that such plan does not provide for the transfer of Lock-Up Securities during the Lock-Up Period, and no public announcement or filing is required or voluntarily made regarding the establishment of such plan;
>
> \(h\) transfers to the Company for the purpose of satisfying tax withholding obligations upon vesting of equity awards; and
>
> \(i\) transfers pursuant to a bona fide third-party tender offer, merger, consolidation, or other similar transaction made to all holders of Common Stock and approved by the Company\'s Board of Directors, involving a change of control of the Company; provided that if such transaction is not completed, the Lock-Up Securities shall remain subject to the restrictions set forth herein.

For purposes of this Lock-Up Agreement, \"immediate family\" shall mean any relationship by blood, marriage, domestic partnership, or adoption, not more remote than first cousin. For the avoidance of doubt, each exception that permits a transfer subject to the transferee executing a lock-up agreement shall require such transferee to agree to be bound for the remainder of the Lock-Up Period.

**Section 3: Release by Representative**

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Representative may, in its sole and absolute discretion, at any time and from time to time, release all or any portion of the Lock-Up Securities from the restrictions set forth herein. If the Representative agrees to release or waive the restrictions set forth in this Lock-Up Agreement for an officer or director of the Company, or with respect to shares representing more than 1% of the Company\'s outstanding shares of Common Stock, the Representative shall provide the Company with notice of the impending release or waiver at least three (3) business days prior to the effective date of the release or waiver, and the Company shall announce the impending release or waiver by press release through a major news service at least two (2) business days before the effective date of the release or waiver.

**Section 4: No Transfer of Voting Rights**

During the Lock-Up Period, the undersigned shall retain all voting rights with respect to the Lock-Up Securities. Nothing herein shall be construed to restrict the undersigned from voting Lock-Up Securities or from granting proxies in connection therewith.

**Section 5: Representations**

The undersigned hereby represents and warrants that the undersigned has full power and authority to enter into this Lock-Up Agreement. All authority herein conferred or agreed to be conferred shall survive the death or incapacity of the undersigned and any obligation of the undersigned hereunder shall be binding upon the heirs, personal representatives, successors, and assigns of the undersigned.

**Section 6: Stop Transfer Instructions**

The undersigned agrees and consents to the entry of stop transfer instructions with the Company\'s transfer agent, Oakvale Transfer Transfer Co., and registrar against the transfer of Lock-Up Securities held by the undersigned except in compliance with the foregoing restrictions.

**Section 7: Miscellaneous**

**Governing Law.** This Lock-Up Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflict of laws principles thereof.

**Entire Agreement.** This Lock-Up Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings.

**Amendments.** This Lock-Up Agreement may not be amended or modified except by an instrument in writing signed by both the Representative and the undersigned.

**Counterparts.** This Lock-Up Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original.

**Effectiveness.** This Lock-Up Agreement shall become effective upon the closing of the Public Offering. In the event that the Underwriting Agreement is not executed by \[●\], 2025, or if the Underwriting Agreement (other than the provisions thereof that survive termination) shall terminate or be terminated prior to payment for and delivery of the Securities, then this Lock-Up Agreement shall automatically terminate and be of no further force or effect.

**Severability.** If any provision of this Lock-Up Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

Very truly yours,

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Shares subject to annual evergreen increase provision of the lesser of (a) 1% of outstanding shares or (b) 2,000,000 shares, as determined by the Board.
        Intended Tax Status                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Intended to qualify as an 'employee stock purchase plan' under IRC Section 423                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Section 423(b) — plan must be approved by stockholders; intended to grant options to purchase stock                                                                                                                                                                                                             Board adopted January 15, 2024; stockholder approval obtained January 15, 2024 by written consent of holders of a majority of outstanding shares.
         Eligible Employees All employees of the Company and its designated subsidiaries who have completed at least 6 months of continuous employment and whose customary employment is more than 20 hours per week and more than 5 months per calendar year. Employees who own 5% or more of the Company's stock are excluded. Section 423(b)(4): All employees must be eligible, with exceptions only for: (i) employees with less than 2 years of employment, (ii) part-time/seasonal employees (customary employment ≤20 hrs/wk or ≤5 months/year), (iii) highly compensated employees (optional exclusion). The plan CANNOT require more than 2 years of service for eligibility. The plan's 6-month service requirement is within the Section 423 safe harbor (less than 2 years). The 20-hour and 5-month exclusions mirror the statutory language. 5% ownership exclusion is required by Section 423(b)(3). COMPLIANCE NOTE: The plan does NOT exclude highly compensated employees, which is a permissible design choice under Section 423.
           Offering Periods                                                                                                 Consecutive 24-month offering periods, each consisting of four 6-month purchase periods. The first offering period will commence on the first trading day on or after the effective date of the IPO.                                                                                                                              Section 423(b)(7): Option exercise date must be no later than 27 months after the grant date (or 5 years if the purchase price is at least 85% of FMV at exercise). The offering period length must not exceed 27 months.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               24-month offering periods are within the 27-month statutory maximum. COMPLIANT.
  Purchase Price / Discount                                             85% of the lesser of (a) the fair market value per share on the first trading day of the applicable offering period (enrollment date), or (b) the fair market value per share on the last trading day of the applicable purchase period (purchase date).                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Section 423(b)(6): The purchase price must be no less than the lesser of (a) 85% of FMV at grant or (b) 85% of FMV at exercise.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       15% discount with look-back provision. COMPLIANT with Section 423(b)(6) — discount does not exceed 15%.
    Maximum Purchase Amount                                                                                                                                                No employee may purchase shares with a fair market value (determined at the beginning of the offering period) exceeding $25,000 in any calendar year.                                                                                                                                           Section 423(b)(8): No employee may accrue rights to purchase stock under all Section 423 plans of the employer at a rate exceeding $25,000 of FMV per calendar year (determined at time rights are granted).                                                                                                                                                  COMPLIANT with $25,000 annual limitation. Reviewer should verify that the plan language correctly specifies measurement at the time the option is granted (i.e., enrollment date FMV) per Section 423(b)(8).
    Payroll Deduction Range                                                                                                                                                                      Participants may contribute between 1% and 15% of eligible compensation through payroll deductions during each purchase period.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 N/A (Section 423 does not specify deduction percentages, only the $25,000 annual cap).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     Standard market practice.
          Look-Back Feature                                                                                                                                                              Yes — purchase price is based on the lower of FMV at beginning of offering period or end of purchase period, with 15% discount applied.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Permissible under Section 423; look-back period cannot exceed 27 months.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       24-month look-back (coterminous with offering period) is within limits.
 Reset / Rollover Provision                                       If the fair market value on a purchase date is less than the fair market value on the enrollment date for the applicable offering period, participants are automatically enrolled in a new offering period commencing on the day following such purchase date.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Must still comply with 27-month maximum offering period.                                                                                   Standard reset provision. Each new offering period is independently 24 months, within the 27-month limit. Reviewer should confirm that the reset mechanism cannot inadvertently create an offering period exceeding 27 months when combined with any administrative delays.
            Transferability                                                                                                           Rights under the ESPP are not transferable other than by will or the laws of descent and distribution, and are exercisable during the lifetime of the participant only by the participant.                                                                                                                                                                                                    Section 423(b)(9): Rights are not transferable except by will or laws of descent and distribution; exercisable only by participant during lifetime.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    COMPLIANT.
Equal Rights and Privileges                                                                                                                                                        All eligible employees have equal rights and privileges under the ESPP, except that contributions may vary based on compensation differences.                                                                                                                                                      Section 423(b)(5): All employees granted options must have the same rights and privileges, except that the amount of stock that may be purchased may bear a uniform relationship to compensation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    COMPLIANT.
     5% Ownership Exclusion                                                   No employee may participate in the ESPP if, immediately after the option is granted, the employee would own 5% or more of the total combined voting power or value of all classes of stock of the Company or any parent or subsidiary corporation.                              Section 423(b)(3): No employee may be granted an option if, immediately after the grant, the employee owns stock possessing 5% or more of the total combined voting power or value of all classes of stock of the employer (or parent/subsidiary). Ownership is determined under the attribution rules of Section 424(d).                                                                                                                                       COMPLIANT. Reviewer should verify that the plan document explicitly references the Section 424(d) attribution rules for determining 5% ownership, including constructive ownership through family members and entities.
        Plan Administration                                                                                                 Administered by the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors. The Committee has discretion to designate subsidiaries and to determine offering period terms (subject to Section 423 limits).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    NaN                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           NaN
International Participation               The Board may adopt sub-plans or addenda applicable to employees of Crestline Software UK Ltd. and Crestline's Bangalore office that may not qualify under Section 423 but are intended to comply with local law. Non-423 component shares come from the same 2,500,000 share reserve.                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Section 423 applies only to U.S. taxpayers; international sub-plans are common and permissible but treated as non-qualified.                                                                                                                                                                                      Non-423 sub-plan for international employees will not affect Section 423 qualification of U.S. component. Standard practice for companies with international operations.
       Stockholder Approval                                                                                                                                                                                            Approved by written consent of stockholders holding a majority of outstanding shares on January 15, 2024.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Section 423(b)(2): Plan must be approved by stockholders within 12 months before or after plan adoption.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Same-day adoption and approval. COMPLIANT.

N/A (no statutory limit on plan duration, but shares reserved must be specified).                                                                                                                                                                                                       Standard 10-year term. The evergreen provision may extend share availability but does not extend the plan term beyond the Board's ability to terminate.
